authors_text: Ming Li,Yusheng Su,Hsiu-Yuan Huang,Jiali Cheng,Xin Hu,Xinmiao Zhang,Huadong Wang,Yujia Qin,Xiaozhi Wang,Kristen A. Lindquist,Zhiyuan Liu,Dan Zhang
title: Language-Specific Representation of Emotion-Concept Knowledge Causally Supports Emotion Inference
title_zh: 情感概念知识的语言特定表示因果支持情感推理
abstract: Humans no doubt use language to communicate about their emotional experiences, but does language in turn help humans understand emotions, or is language just a vehicle of communication? This study used a form of artificial intelligence (AI) known as large language models (LLMs) to assess whether language-based representations of emotion causally contribute to the AI's ability to generate inferences about the emotional meaning of novel situations. Fourteen attributes of human emotion concept representation were found to be represented by the LLM's distinct artificial neuron populations. By manipulating these attribute-related neurons, we in turn demonstrated the role of emotion concept knowledge in generative emotion inference. The attribute-specific performance deterioration was related to the importance of different attributes in human mental space. Our findings provide a proof-in-concept that even a LLM can learn about emotions in the absence of sensory-motor representations and highlight the contribution of language-derived emotion-concept knowledge for emotion inference.
summary: Humans no doubt use language to communicate about their emotional experiences, but does language in turn help humans understand emotions, or is language just a vehicle of communication? This study used a form of artificial intelligence (AI) known as large language models (LLMs) to assess whether language-based representations of emotion causally contribute to the AI's ability to generate inferences about the emotional meaning of novel situations. Fourteen attributes of human emotion concept representation were found to be represented by the LLM's distinct artificial neuron populations. By manipulating these attribute-related neurons, we in turn demonstrated the role of emotion concept knowledge in generative emotion inference. The attribute-specific performance deterioration was related to the importance of different attributes in human mental space. Our findings provide a proof-in-concept that even a LLM can learn about emotions in the absence of sensory-motor representations and highlight the contribution of language-derived emotion-concept knowledge for emotion inference.
abstract_zh: 毫无疑问，人类使用语言来交流情感体验，但语言是否反过来帮助人类理解情感，或者语言只是交流的工具？这项研究使用了一种被称为大语言模型 (LLM) 的人工智能 (AI) 形式来评估基于语言的情感表征是否有助于人工智能对新情境的情感含义进行推断的能力。研究发现，人类情感概念表征的十四个属性是由法学硕士独特的人工神经元群来表征的。通过操纵这些与属性相关的神经元，我们反过来证明了情感概念知识在生成情感推理中的作用。特定属性的表现恶化与人类心理空间中不同属性的重要性有关。我们的研究结果提供了一个概念证明，即即使是法学硕士也可以在缺乏感觉运动表征的情况下学习情感，并强调了语言衍生的情感概念知识对情感推理的贡献。

AI.tldr: 本研究探讨语言特定的情绪概念表示是否因果支持情绪推理，通过大型语言模型（LLMs）分析语言对情绪推断的作用。
AI.motivation: 研究旨在验证语言是否帮助人类理解情绪（而不仅是沟通工具），并探索语言衍生的情绪知识如何促进AI的情绪推理能力。
AI.method: 利用大型语言模型，通过操纵与14个情绪属性相关的神经元，评估其对生成情绪推理任务性能的影响。
AI.result: 发现属性相关神经元被操控后性能下降，且下降程度与人类心智空间中属性的重要性相关，证明语言模型能学习情绪知识。
AI.conclusion: 即使没有感官输入，LLM仍能学习情绪概念知识，语言衍生的情绪表示在情绪推理中起关键作用。

title: Non-separable Covariance Kernels for Spatiotemporal Gaussian Processes based on a Hybrid Spectral Method and the Harmonic Oscillator
title_zh: 基于混合谱法和谐波振荡器的时空高斯过程的不可分离协方差核
abstract: Gaussian processes provide a flexible, non-parametric framework for the approximation of functions in high-dimensional spaces. The covariance kernel is the main engine of Gaussian processes, incorporating correlations that underpin the predictive distribution. For applications with spatiotemporal datasets, suitable kernels should model joint spatial and temporal dependence. Separable space-time covariance kernels offer simplicity and computational efficiency. However, non-separable kernels include space-time interactions that better capture observed correlations. Most non-separable kernels that admit explicit expressions are based on mathematical considerations (admissibility conditions) rather than first-principles derivations. We present a hybrid spectral approach for generating covariance kernels which is based on physical arguments. We use this approach to derive a new class of physically motivated, non-separable covariance kernels which have their roots in the stochastic, linear, damped, harmonic oscillator (LDHO). The new kernels incorporate functions with both monotonic and oscillatory decay of space-time correlations. The LDHO covariance kernels involve space-time interactions which are introduced by dispersion relations that modulate the oscillator coefficients. We derive explicit relations for the spatiotemporal covariance kernels in the three oscillator regimes (underdamping, critical damping, overdamping) and investigate their properties. We further illustrate the hybrid spectral method by deriving covariance kernels that are based on the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck model.
summary: Gaussian processes provide a flexible, non-parametric framework for the approximation of functions in high-dimensional spaces. The covariance kernel is the main engine of Gaussian processes, incorporating correlations that underpin the predictive distribution. For applications with spatiotemporal datasets, suitable kernels should model joint spatial and temporal dependence. Separable space-time covariance kernels offer simplicity and computational efficiency. However, non-separable kernels include space-time interactions that better capture observed correlations. Most non-separable kernels that admit explicit expressions are based on mathematical considerations (admissibility conditions) rather than first-principles derivations. We present a hybrid spectral approach for generating covariance kernels which is based on physical arguments. We use this approach to derive a new class of physically motivated, non-separable covariance kernels which have their roots in the stochastic, linear, damped, harmonic oscillator (LDHO). The new kernels incorporate functions with both monotonic and oscillatory decay of space-time correlations. The LDHO covariance kernels involve space-time interactions which are introduced by dispersion relations that modulate the oscillator coefficients. We derive explicit relations for the spatiotemporal covariance kernels in the three oscillator regimes (underdamping, critical damping, overdamping) and investigate their properties. We further illustrate the hybrid spectral method by deriving covariance kernels that are based on the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck model.
abstract_zh: 高斯过程为高维空间中的函数逼近提供了灵活的非参数框架。协方差核是高斯过程的主要引擎，包含支撑预测分布的相关性。对于具有时空数据集的应用程序，合适的内核应该对联合空间和时间依赖性进行建模。可分离的时空协方差核提供了简单性和计算效率。然而，不可分离的内核包括时空相互作用，可以更好地捕获观察到的相关性。大多数允许显式表达式的不可分离核都是基于数学考虑（可接受性条件）而不是第一性原理推导。我们提出了一种基于物理参数生成协方差核的混合谱方法。我们使用这种方法派生出一类新的物理驱动的、不可分离的协方差核，其根源在于随机、线性、阻尼、谐振子 (LDHO)。新内核结合了具有时空相关性的单调和振荡衰减的函数。 LDHO 协方差核涉及时空相互作用，这些时空相互作用是由调制振荡器系数的色散关系引入的。我们推导了三种振荡状态（欠阻尼、临界阻尼、过阻尼）中时空协方差核的显式关系，并研究了它们的特性。我们通过推导基于 Ornstein-Uhlenbeck 模型的协方差核来进一步说明混合谱方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于混合谱方法和简谐振子模型的非分离时空协方差核，用于改进高维时空数据的高斯过程建模。
AI.motivation: 传统分离核虽计算高效但无法捕捉时空交互作用，现有非分离核多依赖数学条件而非物理原理，缺乏对复杂时空相关性的准确建模。
AI.method: 结合谱方法与阻尼简谐振子（LDHO）物理模型，通过色散关系引入时空耦合，推导出三种阻尼情形下的显式协方差核，并以奥恩斯坦-乌伦贝克模型验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 获得包含单调与振荡衰减特性的新型非分离协方差核，明确其在欠阻尼、临界阻尼、过阻尼三种物理场景下的数学表达式及统计特性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法基于物理机制构建的协方差核能更精确刻画时空数据的相关结构，为高斯过程在复杂时空建模中的应用提供新思路。

search_text: Non-separable Covariance Kernels for Spatiotemporal Gaussian Processes based on a Hybrid Spectral Method and the Harmonic Oscillator 基于混合谱法和谐波振荡器的时空高斯过程的不可分离协方差核 Gaussian processes provide a flexible, non-parametric framework for the approximation of functions in high-dimensional spaces. The covariance kernel is the main engine of Gaussian processes, incorporating correlations that underpin the predictive distribution. For applications with spatiotemporal datasets, suitable kernels should model joint spatial and temporal dependence. Separable space-time covariance kernels offer simplicity and computational efficiency. However, non-separable kernels include space-time interactions that better capture observed correlations. Most non-separable kernels that admit explicit expressions are based on mathematical considerations (admissibility conditions) rather than first-principles derivations. We present a hybrid spectral approach for generating covariance kernels which is based on physical arguments. We use this approach to derive a new class of physically motivated, non-separable covariance kernels which have their roots in the stochastic, linear, damped, harmonic oscillator (LDHO). The new kernels incorporate functions with both monotonic and oscillatory decay of space-time correlations. The LDHO covariance kernels involve space-time interactions which are introduced by dispersion relations that modulate the oscillator coefficients. We derive explicit relations for the spatiotemporal covariance kernels in the three oscillator regimes (underdamping, critical damping, overdamping) and investigate their properties. We further illustrate the hybrid spectral method by deriving covariance kernels that are based on the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck model. 高斯过程为高维空间中的函数逼近提供了灵活的非参数框架。协方差核是高斯过程的主要引擎，包含支撑预测分布的相关性。对于具有时空数据集的应用程序，合适的内核应该对联合空间和时间依赖性进行建模。可分离的时空协方差核提供了简单性和计算效率。然而，不可分离的内核包括时空相互作用，可以更好地捕获观察到的相关性。大多数允许显式表达式的不可分离核都是基于数学考虑（可接受性条件）而不是第一性原理推导。我们提出了一种基于物理参数生成协方差核的混合谱方法。我们使用这种方法派生出一类新的物理驱动的、不可分离的协方差核，其根源在于随机、线性、阻尼、谐振子 (LDHO)。新内核结合了具有时空相关性的单调和振荡衰减的函数。 LDHO 协方差核涉及时空相互作用，这些时空相互作用是由调制振荡器系数的色散关系引入的。我们推导了三种振荡状态（欠阻尼、临界阻尼、过阻尼）中时空协方差核的显式关系，并研究了它们的特性。我们通过推导基于 Ornstein-Uhlenbeck 模型的协方差核来进一步说明混合谱方法。 本文提出基于混合谱方法和简谐振子模型的非分离时空协方差核，用于改进高维时空数据的高斯过程建模。 Dionissios T. Hristopulos stat.ML,cs.AI,cs.LG,math.ST

title: Evaluating Representations with Readout Model Switching
title_zh: 通过读出模型切换评估表示
abstract: Although much of the success of Deep Learning builds on learning good representations, a rigorous method to evaluate their quality is lacking. In this paper, we treat the evaluation of representations as a model selection problem and propose to use the Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle to devise an evaluation metric. Contrary to the established practice of limiting the capacity of the readout model, we design a hybrid discrete and continuous-valued model space for the readout models and employ a switching strategy to combine their predictions. The MDL score takes model complexity, as well as data efficiency into account. As a result, the most appropriate model for the specific task and representation will be chosen, making it a unified measure for comparison. The proposed metric can be efficiently computed with an online method and we present results for pre-trained vision encoders of various architectures (ResNet and ViT) and objective functions (supervised and self-supervised) on a range of downstream tasks. We compare our methods with accuracy-based approaches and show that the latter are inconsistent when multiple readout models are used. Finally, we discuss important properties revealed by our evaluations such as model scaling, preferred readout model, and data efficiency.
summary: Although much of the success of Deep Learning builds on learning good representations, a rigorous method to evaluate their quality is lacking. In this paper, we treat the evaluation of representations as a model selection problem and propose to use the Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle to devise an evaluation metric. Contrary to the established practice of limiting the capacity of the readout model, we design a hybrid discrete and continuous-valued model space for the readout models and employ a switching strategy to combine their predictions. The MDL score takes model complexity, as well as data efficiency into account. As a result, the most appropriate model for the specific task and representation will be chosen, making it a unified measure for comparison. The proposed metric can be efficiently computed with an online method and we present results for pre-trained vision encoders of various architectures (ResNet and ViT) and objective functions (supervised and self-supervised) on a range of downstream tasks. We compare our methods with accuracy-based approaches and show that the latter are inconsistent when multiple readout models are used. Finally, we discuss important properties revealed by our evaluations such as model scaling, preferred readout model, and data efficiency.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度学习的成功很大程度上建立在学习良好的表征的基础上，但缺乏评估其质量的严格方法。在本文中，我们将表示的评估视为模型选择问题，并建议使用最小描述长度（MDL）原则来设计评估指标。与限制读出模型容量的既定做法相反，我们为读出模型设计了混合离散和连续值模型空间，并采用切换策略来组合它们的预测。 MDL 分数考虑了模型复杂性以及数据效率。因此，将选择最适合特定任务和表示的模型，使其成为用于比较的统一衡量标准。所提出的指标可以通过在线方法有效地计算，并且我们在一系列下游任务上展示了各种架构（ResNet 和 ViT）的预训练视觉编码器和目标函数（监督和自监督）的结果。我们将我们的方法与基于准确性的方法进行比较，结果表明，当使用多个读出模型时，后者是不一致的。最后，我们讨论了我们的评估所揭示的重要属性，例如模型缩放、首选读出模型和数据效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于最小描述长度（MDL）原则的表示评估方法，通过读出模型切换策略选择最适合特定任务和表示的模型，实现统一的表示质量评价。
AI.motivation: 深度学习的成功依赖于学习良好的表示，但缺乏严谨的表示质量评估方法，现有基于准确率的方法在多模型场景下存在不一致性。
AI.method: 将表示评估转化为模型选择问题，设计离散与连续混合的读出模型空间，结合切换策略融合预测结果，基于MDL原则综合考虑模型复杂度和数据效率。
AI.result: 提出的度量方法能高效计算，实验表明在多种预训练视觉编码器（ResNet/ViT）和下游任务中优于基于准确率的方法，并揭示了模型扩展性、最优读出模型选择等特性。
AI.conclusion: MDL-based评估框架为表示质量提供了统一衡量标准，揭示了模型规模与数据效率之间的关键关系，为后续研究提供了新视角。

title: On Feasibility of Server-side Backdoor Attacks on Split Learning
title_zh: 分裂学习服务器端后门攻击的可行性研究
abstract: Split learning is a collaborative learning design that allows several participants (clients) to train a shared model while keeping their datasets private. Recent studies demonstrate that collaborative learning models, specifically federated learning, are vulnerable to security and privacy attacks such as model inference and backdoor attacks. Backdoor attacks are a group of poisoning attacks in which the attacker tries to control the model output by manipulating the model's training process. While there have been studies regarding inference attacks on split learning, it has not yet been tested for backdoor attacks. This paper performs a novel backdoor attack on split learning and studies its effectiveness. Despite traditional backdoor attacks done on the client side, we inject the backdoor trigger from the server side. For this purpose, we provide two attack methods: one using a surrogate client and another using an autoencoder to poison the model via incoming smashed data and its outgoing gradient toward the innocent participants. We did our experiments using three model architectures and three publicly available datasets in the image domain and ran a total of 761 experiments to evaluate our attack methods. The results show that despite using strong patterns and injection methods, split learning is highly robust and resistant to such poisoning attacks. While we get the attack success rate of 100% as our best result for the MNIST dataset, in most of the other cases, our attack shows little success when increasing the cut layer.
summary: Split learning is a collaborative learning design that allows several participants (clients) to train a shared model while keeping their datasets private. Recent studies demonstrate that collaborative learning models, specifically federated learning, are vulnerable to security and privacy attacks such as model inference and backdoor attacks. Backdoor attacks are a group of poisoning attacks in which the attacker tries to control the model output by manipulating the model's training process. While there have been studies regarding inference attacks on split learning, it has not yet been tested for backdoor attacks. This paper performs a novel backdoor attack on split learning and studies its effectiveness. Despite traditional backdoor attacks done on the client side, we inject the backdoor trigger from the server side. For this purpose, we provide two attack methods: one using a surrogate client and another using an autoencoder to poison the model via incoming smashed data and its outgoing gradient toward the innocent participants. We did our experiments using three model architectures and three publicly available datasets in the image domain and ran a total of 761 experiments to evaluate our attack methods. The results show that despite using strong patterns and injection methods, split learning is highly robust and resistant to such poisoning attacks. While we get the attack success rate of 100% as our best result for the MNIST dataset, in most of the other cases, our attack shows little success when increasing the cut layer.
abstract_zh: 分割学习是一种协作学习设计，允许多个参与者（客户端）训练共享模型，同时保持数据集的私密性。最近的研究表明，协作学习模型，特别是联邦学习，很容易受到安全和隐私攻击，例如模型推理和后门攻击。后门攻击是一组中毒攻击，攻击者试图通过操纵模型的训练过程来控制模型输出。虽然已经有关于分割学习的推理攻击的研究，但尚未针对后门攻击进行测试。本文对分裂学习进行了一种新颖的后门攻击并研究了其有效性。尽管传统的后门攻击是在客户端进行的，但我们从服务器端注入后门触发器。为此，我们提供了两种攻击方法：一种使用代理客户端，另一种使用自动编码器通过传入的粉碎数据及其向无辜参与者的传出梯度来毒害模型。我们使用图像领域的三种模型架构和三个公开可用的数据集进行了实验，并总共运行了 761 次实验来评估我们的攻击方法。结果表明，尽管使用了强大的模式和注入方法，分割学习仍然具有很强的鲁棒性，并且能够抵抗此类中毒攻击。虽然我们获得 100% 的攻击成功率作为 MNIST 数据集的最佳结果，但在大多数其他情况下，我们的攻击在增加剪切层时几乎没有成功。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在分裂学习框架中，从服务器端发起后门攻击的可行性，并评估其效果。
AI.motivation: 尽管联邦学习已被证明易受后门攻击，但分裂学习的后门攻击尚未被研究。本文旨在填补这一空白，探索服务器端攻击的可能性。
AI.method: 提出两种攻击方法：通过代理客户端和自编码器，利用输入数据和梯度污染模型。在三个图像数据集和三种模型架构上进行了761次实验。
AI.result: 在MNIST数据集上实现100%攻击成功率，但其他情况下随着切割层数增加效果显著下降，表明分裂学习对污染攻击具有较高鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 分裂学习对服务器端后门攻击具有较强抵抗力，但研究揭示了其潜在安全风险，需进一步防御机制研究。

search_text: On Feasibility of Server-side Backdoor Attacks on Split Learning 分裂学习服务器端后门攻击的可行性研究 Split learning is a collaborative learning design that allows several participants (clients) to train a shared model while keeping their datasets private. Recent studies demonstrate that collaborative learning models, specifically federated learning, are vulnerable to security and privacy attacks such as model inference and backdoor attacks. Backdoor attacks are a group of poisoning attacks in which the attacker tries to control the model output by manipulating the model's training process. While there have been studies regarding inference attacks on split learning, it has not yet been tested for backdoor attacks. This paper performs a novel backdoor attack on split learning and studies its effectiveness. Despite traditional backdoor attacks done on the client side, we inject the backdoor trigger from the server side. For this purpose, we provide two attack methods: one using a surrogate client and another using an autoencoder to poison the model via incoming smashed data and its outgoing gradient toward the innocent participants. We did our experiments using three model architectures and three publicly available datasets in the image domain and ran a total of 761 experiments to evaluate our attack methods. The results show that despite using strong patterns and injection methods, split learning is highly robust and resistant to such poisoning attacks. While we get the attack success rate of 100% as our best result for the MNIST dataset, in most of the other cases, our attack shows little success when increasing the cut layer. 分割学习是一种协作学习设计，允许多个参与者（客户端）训练共享模型，同时保持数据集的私密性。最近的研究表明，协作学习模型，特别是联邦学习，很容易受到安全和隐私攻击，例如模型推理和后门攻击。后门攻击是一组中毒攻击，攻击者试图通过操纵模型的训练过程来控制模型输出。虽然已经有关于分割学习的推理攻击的研究，但尚未针对后门攻击进行测试。本文对分裂学习进行了一种新颖的后门攻击并研究了其有效性。尽管传统的后门攻击是在客户端进行的，但我们从服务器端注入后门触发器。为此，我们提供了两种攻击方法：一种使用代理客户端，另一种使用自动编码器通过传入的粉碎数据及其向无辜参与者的传出梯度来毒害模型。我们使用图像领域的三种模型架构和三个公开可用的数据集进行了实验，并总共运行了 761 次实验来评估我们的攻击方法。结果表明，尽管使用了强大的模式和注入方法，分割学习仍然具有很强的鲁棒性，并且能够抵抗此类中毒攻击。虽然我们获得 100% 的攻击成功率作为 MNIST 数据集的最佳结果，但在大多数其他情况下，我们的攻击在增加剪切层时几乎没有成功。 本文研究了在分裂学习框架中，从服务器端发起后门攻击的可行性，并评估其效果。 Behrad Tajalli,Oguzhan Ersoy,Stjepan Picek cs.CR,cs.AI

title: Rethinking Data-Free Quantization as a Zero-Sum Game
title_zh: 重新思考无数据量化作为零和游戏
abstract: Data-free quantization (DFQ) recovers the performance of quantized network (Q) without accessing the real data, but generates the fake sample via a generator (G) by learning from full-precision network (P) instead. However, such sample generation process is totally independent of Q, specialized as failing to consider the adaptability of the generated samples, i.e., beneficial or adversarial, over the learning process of Q, resulting into non-ignorable performance loss. Building on this, several crucial questions -- how to measure and exploit the sample adaptability to Q under varied bit-width scenarios? how to generate the samples with desirable adaptability to benefit the quantized network? -- impel us to revisit DFQ. In this paper, we answer the above questions from a game-theory perspective to specialize DFQ as a zero-sum game between two players -- a generator and a quantized network, and further propose an Adaptability-aware Sample Generation (AdaSG) method. Technically, AdaSG reformulates DFQ as a dynamic maximization-vs-minimization game process anchored on the sample adaptability. The maximization process aims to generate the sample with desirable adaptability, such sample adaptability is further reduced by the minimization process after calibrating Q for performance recovery. The Balance Gap is defined to guide the stationarity of the game process to maximally benefit Q. The theoretical analysis and empirical studies verify the superiority of AdaSG over the state-of-the-arts. Our code is available at https://github.com/hfutqian/AdaSG.
summary: Data-free quantization (DFQ) recovers the performance of quantized network (Q) without accessing the real data, but generates the fake sample via a generator (G) by learning from full-precision network (P) instead. However, such sample generation process is totally independent of Q, specialized as failing to consider the adaptability of the generated samples, i.e., beneficial or adversarial, over the learning process of Q, resulting into non-ignorable performance loss. Building on this, several crucial questions -- how to measure and exploit the sample adaptability to Q under varied bit-width scenarios? how to generate the samples with desirable adaptability to benefit the quantized network? -- impel us to revisit DFQ. In this paper, we answer the above questions from a game-theory perspective to specialize DFQ as a zero-sum game between two players -- a generator and a quantized network, and further propose an Adaptability-aware Sample Generation (AdaSG) method. Technically, AdaSG reformulates DFQ as a dynamic maximization-vs-minimization game process anchored on the sample adaptability. The maximization process aims to generate the sample with desirable adaptability, such sample adaptability is further reduced by the minimization process after calibrating Q for performance recovery. The Balance Gap is defined to guide the stationarity of the game process to maximally benefit Q. The theoretical analysis and empirical studies verify the superiority of AdaSG over the state-of-the-arts. Our code is available at https://github.com/hfutqian/AdaSG.
abstract_zh: 无数据量化（DFQ）在不访问真实数据的情况下恢复了量化网络（Q）的性能，而是通过生成器（G）通过从全精度网络（P）学习来生成假样本。然而，这样的样本生成过程完全独立于Q，特别是没有考虑生成的样本对Q的学习过程的适应性，即有利或不利，导致不可忽略的性能损失。在此基础上，提出了几个关键问题——如何测量和利用样本在不同位宽场景下对 Q 的适应性？如何生成具有理想适应性的样本以使量化网络受益？ ——促使我们重新审视DFQ。在本文中，我们从博弈论的角度回答了上述问题，将 DFQ 专门化为两个参与者（生成器和量化网络）之间的零和博弈，并进一步提出了一种自适应感知样本生成（AdaSG）方法。从技术上讲，AdaSG 将 DFQ 重新表述为基于样本适应性的动态最大化与最小化博弈过程。最大化过程的目的是生成具有理想适应性的样本，在校准Q以恢复性能后，通过最小化过程进一步降低样本的适应性。定义平衡间隙是为了引导博弈过程的平稳性，以最大程度地使 Q 受益。理论分析和实证研究验证了 AdaSG 相对于现有技术的优越性。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/hfutqian/AdaSG 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文将数据无关量化（DFQ）重新定义为生成器与量化网络之间的零和博弈，提出适应性感知样本生成方法AdaSG，以提升量化网络性能。
AI.motivation: 传统DFQ方法生成的样本未考虑其对量化网络的适应性（有益或对抗），导致性能损失，需通过衡量和利用样本适应性来优化DFQ。
AI.method: 将DFQ建模为动态最大化-最小化博弈过程，定义平衡差距（Balance Gap）指导博弈稳定性，生成器生成高适应性样本后通过量化网络校准进一步优化。
AI.result: 理论分析与实验验证表明，AdaSG在多种位宽场景下优于现有方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 通过博弈论视角引入样本适应性机制，AdaSG有效解决了DFQ中样本与量化网络适配性不足的问题，实现了更优的性能恢复。

authors_text: MinJin Hwang,Bappaditya Dey,Enrique Dehaerne,Sandip Halder,Young-han Shin
title: SEMI-PointRend: Improved Semiconductor Wafer Defect Classification and Segmentation as Rendering
title_zh: SEMI-PointRend：改进的半导体晶圆缺陷分类和分割渲染
comment: 7 pages, 6 figures, 5 tables. To be published by SPIE in the proceedings of Metrology, Inspection, and Process Control XXXVII
comments: 7 pages, 6 figures, 5 tables. To be published by SPIE in the proceedings of Metrology, Inspection, and Process Control XXXVII
abstract: In this study, we applied the PointRend (Point-based Rendering) method to semiconductor defect segmentation. PointRend is an iterative segmentation algorithm inspired by image rendering in computer graphics, a new image segmentation method that can generate high-resolution segmentation masks. It can also be flexibly integrated into common instance segmentation meta-architecture such as Mask-RCNN and semantic meta-architecture such as FCN. We implemented a model, termed as SEMI-PointRend, to generate precise segmentation masks by applying the PointRend neural network module. In this paper, we focus on comparing the defect segmentation predictions of SEMI-PointRend and Mask-RCNN for various defect types (line-collapse, single bridge, thin bridge, multi bridge non-horizontal). We show that SEMI-PointRend can outperforms Mask R-CNN by up to 18.8% in terms of segmentation mean average precision.
summary: In this study, we applied the PointRend (Point-based Rendering) method to semiconductor defect segmentation. PointRend is an iterative segmentation algorithm inspired by image rendering in computer graphics, a new image segmentation method that can generate high-resolution segmentation masks. It can also be flexibly integrated into common instance segmentation meta-architecture such as Mask-RCNN and semantic meta-architecture such as FCN. We implemented a model, termed as SEMI-PointRend, to generate precise segmentation masks by applying the PointRend neural network module. In this paper, we focus on comparing the defect segmentation predictions of SEMI-PointRend and Mask-RCNN for various defect types (line-collapse, single bridge, thin bridge, multi bridge non-horizontal). We show that SEMI-PointRend can outperforms Mask R-CNN by up to 18.8% in terms of segmentation mean average precision.
abstract_zh: 在本研究中，我们将PointRend（基于点的渲染）方法应用于半导体缺陷分割。 PointRend 是一种迭代分割算法，其灵感来自计算机图形学中的图像渲染，是一种可以生成高分辨率分割掩模的新图像分割方法。它还可以灵活地集成到常见的实例分割元架构（例如Mask-RCNN）和语义元架构（例如FCN）中。我们实现了一个名为 SEMI-PointRend 的模型，通过应用 PointRend 神经网络模块来生成精确的分割掩模。在本文中，我们重点比较 SEMI-PointRend 和 Mask-RCNN 对于各种缺陷类型（线塌陷、单桥、细桥、多桥非水平）的缺陷分割预测。我们表明，在分割平均精度方面，SEMI-PointRend 的性能比 Mask R-CNN 高出 18.8%。

AI.tldr: 本文将PointRend方法应用于半导体晶圆缺陷分割，提出SEMI-PointRend模型以提升分割精度。
AI.motivation: 传统分割方法在处理半导体晶圆复杂缺陷时存在精度不足的问题，需更精确的分割算法以满足工业需求。
AI.method: 基于PointRend算法设计SEMI-PointRend模型，结合Mask-RCNN等架构，通过迭代生成高分辨率分割掩码。
AI.result: 在四种缺陷类型上，SEMI-PointRend较Mask-RCNN的分割平均精度提升最高达18.8%。

search_text: SEMI-PointRend: Improved Semiconductor Wafer Defect Classification and Segmentation as Rendering SEMI-PointRend：改进的半导体晶圆缺陷分类和分割渲染 In this study, we applied the PointRend (Point-based Rendering) method to semiconductor defect segmentation. PointRend is an iterative segmentation algorithm inspired by image rendering in computer graphics, a new image segmentation method that can generate high-resolution segmentation masks. It can also be flexibly integrated into common instance segmentation meta-architecture such as Mask-RCNN and semantic meta-architecture such as FCN. We implemented a model, termed as SEMI-PointRend, to generate precise segmentation masks by applying the PointRend neural network module. In this paper, we focus on comparing the defect segmentation predictions of SEMI-PointRend and Mask-RCNN for various defect types (line-collapse, single bridge, thin bridge, multi bridge non-horizontal). We show that SEMI-PointRend can outperforms Mask R-CNN by up to 18.8% in terms of segmentation mean average precision. 在本研究中，我们将PointRend（基于点的渲染）方法应用于半导体缺陷分割。 PointRend 是一种迭代分割算法，其灵感来自计算机图形学中的图像渲染，是一种可以生成高分辨率分割掩模的新图像分割方法。它还可以灵活地集成到常见的实例分割元架构（例如Mask-RCNN）和语义元架构（例如FCN）中。我们实现了一个名为 SEMI-PointRend 的模型，通过应用 PointRend 神经网络模块来生成精确的分割掩模。在本文中，我们重点比较 SEMI-PointRend 和 Mask-RCNN 对于各种缺陷类型（线塌陷、单桥、细桥、多桥非水平）的缺陷分割预测。我们表明，在分割平均精度方面，SEMI-PointRend 的性能比 Mask R-CNN 高出 18.8%。 本文将PointRend方法应用于半导体晶圆缺陷分割，提出SEMI-PointRend模型以提升分割精度。 MinJin Hwang,Bappaditya Dey,Enrique Dehaerne,Sandip Halder,Young-han Shin cs.CV

authors_text: Enrique Dehaerne,Bappaditya Dey,Sandip Halder,Stefan De Gendt
title: Optimizing YOLOv7 for Semiconductor Defect Detection
title_zh: 优化 YOLOv7 以进行半导体缺陷检测
comment: 8 pages, 4 figures, 5 tables. To be published by SPIE in the proceedings of Metrology, Inspection, and Process Control XXXVII
comments: 8 pages, 4 figures, 5 tables. To be published by SPIE in the proceedings of Metrology, Inspection, and Process Control XXXVII
abstract: The field of object detection using Deep Learning (DL) is constantly evolving with many new techniques and models being proposed. YOLOv7 is a state-of-the-art object detector based on the YOLO family of models which have become popular for industrial applications. One such possible application domain can be semiconductor defect inspection. The performance of any machine learning model depends on its hyperparameters. Furthermore, combining predictions of one or more models in different ways can also affect performance. In this research, we experiment with YOLOv7, a recently proposed, state-of-the-art object detector, by training and evaluating models with different hyperparameters to investigate which ones improve performance in terms of detection precision for semiconductor line space pattern defects. The base YOLOv7 model with default hyperparameters and Non Maximum Suppression (NMS) prediction combining outperforms all RetinaNet models from previous work in terms of mean Average Precision (mAP). We find that vertically flipping images randomly during training yields a 3% improvement in the mean AP of all defect classes. Other hyperparameter values improved AP only for certain classes compared to the default model. Combining models that achieve the best AP for different defect classes was found to be an effective ensembling strategy. Combining predictions from ensembles using Weighted Box Fusion (WBF) prediction gave the best performance. The best ensemble with WBF improved on the mAP of the default model by 10%.

YOLOv7 is a state-of-the-art object detector based on the YOLO family of models which have become popular for industrial applications. One such possible application domain can be semiconductor defect inspection. The performance of any machine learning model depends on its hyperparameters. Furthermore, combining predictions of one or more models in different ways can also affect performance. In this research, we experiment with YOLOv7, a recently proposed, state-of-the-art object detector, by training and evaluating models with different hyperparameters to investigate which ones improve performance in terms of detection precision for semiconductor line space pattern defects. The base YOLOv7 model with default hyperparameters and Non Maximum Suppression (NMS) prediction combining outperforms all RetinaNet models from previous work in terms of mean Average Precision (mAP). We find that vertically flipping images randomly during training yields a 3% improvement in the mean AP of all defect classes. Other hyperparameter values improved AP only for certain classes compared to the default model. Combining models that achieve the best AP for different defect classes was found to be an effective ensembling strategy. Combining predictions from ensembles using Weighted Box Fusion (WBF) prediction gave the best performance. The best ensemble with WBF improved on the mAP of the default model by 10%.
abstract_zh: 使用深度学习 (DL) 的物体检测领域不断发展，提出了许多新技术和模型。 YOLOv7 是一款基于 YOLO 系列模型的最先进的物体检测器，该系列模型已在工业应用中广受欢迎。其中一种可能的应用领域是半导体缺陷检查。任何机器学习模型的性能都取决于其超参数。此外，以不同方式组合一个或多个模型的预测也会影响性能。在这项研究中，我们使用最近提出的最先进的目标检测器 YOLOv7 进行实验，通过训练和评估具有不同超参数的模型来研究哪些模型可以提高半导体线空间图案缺陷检测精度的性能。具有默认超参数和非最大抑制 (NMS) 预测的基本 YOLOv7 模型在平均精度 (mAP) 方面优于之前工作中的所有 RetinaNet 模型。我们发现，在训练期间随机垂直翻转图像可使所有缺陷类别的平均 AP 提高 3%。与默认模型相比，其他超参数值仅针对某些类别改进了 AP。人们发现，组合针对不同缺陷类别实现最佳 AP 的模型是一种有效的集成策略。使用加权框融合 (WBF) 预测组合来自集成的预测可提供最佳性能。具有 WBF 的最佳集成比默认模型的 mAP 提高了 10%。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过调整超参数和模型集成策略，优化YOLOv7模型在半导体线宽图案缺陷检测中的性能。
AI.motivation: 半导体缺陷检测对产品质量至关重要，而YOLOv7作为先进目标检测模型，其性能可通过超参数调优和模型集成进一步提升。
AI.method: 实验对比不同超参数配置，采用随机垂直翻转增强数据，并通过加权框融合（WBF）集成多模型预测结果。
AI.result: 垂直翻转训练提升整体mAP 3%，最佳集成模型（WBF）使mAP较默认模型提升10%，优于先前RetinaNet模型。
AI.conclusion: 超参数优化与模型集成策略显著提升YOLOv7在半导体缺陷检测中的精度，为工业检测提供有效方法。

search_text: Optimizing YOLOv7 for Semiconductor Defect Detection 优化 YOLOv7 以进行半导体缺陷检测 The field of object detection using Deep Learning (DL) is constantly evolving with many new techniques and models being proposed. YOLOv7 is a state-of-the-art object detector based on the YOLO family of models which have become popular for industrial applications. One such possible application domain can be semiconductor defect inspection. The performance of any machine learning model depends on its hyperparameters. Furthermore, combining predictions of one or more models in different ways can also affect performance. In this research, we experiment with YOLOv7, a recently proposed, state-of-the-art object detector, by training and evaluating models with different hyperparameters to investigate which ones improve performance in terms of detection precision for semiconductor line space pattern defects. The base YOLOv7 model with default hyperparameters and Non Maximum Suppression (NMS) prediction combining outperforms all RetinaNet models from previous work in terms of mean Average Precision (mAP). We find that vertically flipping images randomly during training yields a 3% improvement in the mean AP of all defect classes. Other hyperparameter values improved AP only for certain classes compared to the default model. Combining models that achieve the best AP for different defect classes was found to be an effective ensembling strategy. Combining predictions from ensembles using Weighted Box Fusion (WBF) prediction gave the best performance. The best ensemble with WBF improved on the mAP of the default model by 10%. 使用深度学习 (DL) 的物体检测领域不断发展，提出了许多新技术和模型。 YOLOv7 是一款基于 YOLO 系列模型的最先进的物体检测器，该系列模型已在工业应用中广受欢迎。其中一种可能的应用领域是半导体缺陷检查。任何机器学习模型的性能都取决于其超参数。此外，以不同方式组合一个或多个模型的预测也会影响性能。在这项研究中，我们使用最近提出的最先进的目标检测器 YOLOv7 进行实验，通过训练和评估具有不同超参数的模型来研究哪些模型可以提高半导体线空间图案缺陷检测精度的性能。具有默认超参数和非最大抑制 (NMS) 预测的基本 YOLOv7 模型在平均精度 (mAP) 方面优于之前工作中的所有 RetinaNet 模型。我们发现，在训练期间随机垂直翻转图像可使所有缺陷类别的平均 AP 提高 3%。与默认模型相比，其他超参数值仅针对某些类别改进了 AP。人们发现，组合针对不同缺陷类别实现最佳 AP 的模型是一种有效的集成策略。使用加权框融合 (WBF) 预测组合来自集成的预测可提供最佳性能。具有 WBF 的最佳集成比默认模型的 mAP 提高了 10%。 本文旨在通过调整超参数和模型集成策略，优化YOLOv7模型在半导体线宽图案缺陷检测中的性能。 Enrique Dehaerne,Bappaditya Dey,Sandip Halder,Stefan De Gendt cs.CV

authors_text: Yuan-Chia Cheng,Zu-Yun Shiau,Fu-En Yang,Yu-Chiang Frank Wang
title: TAX: Tendency-and-Assignment Explainer for Semantic Segmentation with Multi-Annotators
title_zh: TAX：使用多注释器进行语义分割的趋势和分配解释器
abstract: To understand how deep neural networks perform classification predictions, recent research attention has been focusing on developing techniques to offer desirable explanations. However, most existing methods cannot be easily applied for semantic segmentation; moreover, they are not designed to offer interpretability under the multi-annotator setting. Instead of viewing ground-truth pixel-level labels annotated by a single annotator with consistent labeling tendency, we aim at providing interpretable semantic segmentation and answer two critical yet practical questions: "who" contributes to the resulting segmentation, and "why" such an assignment is determined. In this paper, we present a learning framework of Tendency-and-Assignment Explainer (TAX), designed to offer interpretability at the annotator and assignment levels. More specifically, we learn convolution kernel subsets for modeling labeling tendencies of each type of annotation, while a prototype bank is jointly observed to offer visual guidance for learning the above kernels. For evaluation, we consider both synthetic and real-world datasets with multi-annotators. We show that our TAX can be applied to state-of-the-art network architectures with comparable performances, while segmentation interpretability at both levels can be offered accordingly.
summary: To understand how deep neural networks perform classification predictions, recent research attention has been focusing on developing techniques to offer desirable explanations. However, most existing methods cannot be easily applied for semantic segmentation; moreover, they are not designed to offer interpretability under the multi-annotator setting. Instead of viewing ground-truth pixel-level labels annotated by a single annotator with consistent labeling tendency, we aim at providing interpretable semantic segmentation and answer two critical yet practical questions: "who" contributes to the resulting segmentation, and "why" such an assignment is determined. In this paper, we present a learning framework of Tendency-and-Assignment Explainer (TAX), designed to offer interpretability at the annotator and assignment levels. More specifically, we learn convolution kernel subsets for modeling labeling tendencies of each type of annotation, while a prototype bank is jointly observed to offer visual guidance for learning the above kernels. For evaluation, we consider both synthetic and real-world datasets with multi-annotators. We show that our TAX can be applied to state-of-the-art network architectures with comparable performances, while segmentation interpretability at both levels can be offered accordingly.
abstract_zh: 为了了解深度神经网络如何执行分类预测，最近的研究注意力一直集中在开发提供理想解释的技术上。然而，大多数现有方法不能轻易应用于语义分割；此外，它们并不是为了在多注释器设置下提供可解释性而设计的。我们不是查看由具有一致标签趋势的单个注释器注释的地面实况像素级标签，而是旨在提供可解释的语义分割并回答两个关键但实际的问题：“谁”对最终的分割做出了贡献，以及“为什么”确定这样的分配。在本文中，我们提出了一个趋势和分配解释器（TAX）的学习框架，旨在提供注释器和分配级别的可解释性。更具体地说，我们学习卷积核子集来对每种注释类型的标记趋势进行建模，同时联合观察原型库以提供学习上述内核的视觉指导。为了进行评估，我们考虑了具有多注释器的合成数据集和真实数据集。我们表明，我们的 TAX 可以应用于具有可比性能的最先进的网络架构，同时可以相应地提供两个级别的分段可解释性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TAX框架，用于在多标注者设置下实现语义分割的可解释性，解决现有方法无法处理多标注者场景的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习解释方法难以应用于语义分割任务，且未考虑多标注者场景下的可解释性需求，无法回答‘谁贡献了分割结果’及‘为何如此分配’的问题。
AI.method: 通过学习卷积核子集建模每类标注的标签倾向性，并结合原型库提供视觉指导，实现标注者和分配层级的可解释性。
AI.result: 在合成与真实数据集上验证了TAX可应用于先进网络架构，同时提供双层级的分割可解释性，性能表现与现有方法相当。
AI.conclusion: TAX有效解决了多标注者语义分割的可解释性问题，为模型决策提供了标注者和分配层级的双重解释依据。

search_text: TAX: Tendency-and-Assignment Explainer for Semantic Segmentation with Multi-Annotators TAX：使用多注释器进行语义分割的趋势和分配解释器 To understand how deep neural networks perform classification predictions, recent research attention has been focusing on developing techniques to offer desirable explanations. However, most existing methods cannot be easily applied for semantic segmentation; moreover, they are not designed to offer interpretability under the multi-annotator setting. Instead of viewing ground-truth pixel-level labels annotated by a single annotator with consistent labeling tendency, we aim at providing interpretable semantic segmentation and answer two critical yet practical questions: "who" contributes to the resulting segmentation, and "why" such an assignment is determined. In this paper, we present a learning framework of Tendency-and-Assignment Explainer (TAX), designed to offer interpretability at the annotator and assignment levels. More specifically, we learn convolution kernel subsets for modeling labeling tendencies of each type of annotation, while a prototype bank is jointly observed to offer visual guidance for learning the above kernels. For evaluation, we consider both synthetic and real-world datasets with multi-annotators. We show that our TAX can be applied to state-of-the-art network architectures with comparable performances, while segmentation interpretability at both levels can be offered accordingly. 为了了解深度神经网络如何执行分类预测，最近的研究注意力一直集中在开发提供理想解释的技术上。然而，大多数现有方法不能轻易应用于语义分割；此外，它们并不是为了在多注释器设置下提供可解释性而设计的。我们不是查看由具有一致标签趋势的单个注释器注释的地面实况像素级标签，而是旨在提供可解释的语义分割并回答两个关键但实际的问题：“谁”对最终的分割做出了贡献，以及“为什么”确定这样的分配。在本文中，我们提出了一个趋势和分配解释器（TAX）的学习框架，旨在提供注释器和分配级别的可解释性。更具体地说，我们学习卷积核子集来对每种注释类型的标记趋势进行建模，同时联合观察原型库以提供学习上述内核的视觉指导。为了进行评估，我们考虑了具有多注释器的合成数据集和真实数据集。我们表明，我们的 TAX 可以应用于具有可比性能的最先进的网络架构，同时可以相应地提供两个级别的分段可解释性。 本文提出TAX框架，用于在多标注者设置下实现语义分割的可解释性，解决现有方法无法处理多标注者场景的问题。 Yuan-Chia Cheng,Zu-Yun Shiau,Fu-En Yang,Yu-Chiang Frank Wang cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

title: Deep Selector-JPEG: Adaptive JPEG Image Compression for Computer Vision in Image classification with Human Vision Criteria
title_zh: Deep Selector-JPEG：用于具有人类视觉标准的图像分类中的计算机视觉的自适应 JPEG 图像压缩
abstract: With limited storage/bandwidth resources, input images to Computer Vision (CV) applications that use Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) are often encoded with JPEG that is tailored to Human Vision (HV). This paper presents Deep Selector-JPEG, an adaptive JPEG compression method that targets image classification while satisfying HV criteria. For each image, Deep Selector-JPEG selects adaptively a Quality Factor (QF) to compress the image so that a good trade-off between the Compression Ratio (CR) and DNN classifier Accuracy (Rate-Accuracy performance) can be achieved over a set of images for a variety of DNN classifiers while the MS-SSIM of such compressed image is greater than a threshold value predetermined by HV with a high probability. Deep Selector-JPEG is designed via light-weighted or heavy-weighted selector architectures. Experimental results show that in comparison with JPEG at the same CR, Deep Selector-JPEG achieves better Rate-Accuracy performance over the ImageNet validation set for all tested DNN classifiers with gains in classification accuracy between 0.2% and 1% at the same CRs while satisfying HV constraints. Deep Selector-JPEG can also roughly provide the original classification accuracy at higher CRs.
summary: With limited storage/bandwidth resources, input images to Computer Vision (CV) applications that use Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) are often encoded with JPEG that is tailored to Human Vision (HV). This paper presents Deep Selector-JPEG, an adaptive JPEG compression method that targets image classification while satisfying HV criteria. For each image, Deep Selector-JPEG selects adaptively a Quality Factor (QF) to compress the image so that a good trade-off between the Compression Ratio (CR) and DNN classifier Accuracy (Rate-Accuracy performance) can be achieved over a set of images for a variety of DNN classifiers while the MS-SSIM of such compressed image is greater than a threshold value predetermined by HV with a high probability. Deep Selector-JPEG is designed via light-weighted or heavy-weighted selector architectures. Experimental results show that in comparison with JPEG at the same CR, Deep Selector-JPEG achieves better Rate-Accuracy performance over the ImageNet validation set for all tested DNN classifiers with gains in classification accuracy between 0.2% and 1% at the same CRs while satisfying HV constraints. Deep Selector-JPEG can also roughly provide the original classification accuracy at higher CRs.
abstract_zh: 由于存储/带宽资源有限，使用深度神经网络 (DNN) 的计算机视觉 (CV) 应用程序的输入图像通常使用专为人类视觉 (HV) 定制的 JPEG 进行编码。本文提出了 Deep Selector-JPEG，这是一种自适应 JPEG 压缩方法，其目标是图像分类，同时满足 HV 标准。对于每幅图像，Deep Selector-JPEG 自适应地选择一个质量因子（QF）来压缩图像，从而可以在多种 DNN 分类器的一组图像上实现压缩比（CR）和 DNN 分类器精度（速率-精度性能）之间的良好权衡，而这种压缩图像的 MS-SSIM 大概率大于 HV 预先确定的阈值。 Deep Selector-JPEG 是通过轻量级或重量级选择器架构设计的。实验结果表明，与相同 CR 下的 JPEG 相比，Deep Selector-JPEG 在所有测试的 DNN 分类器的 ImageNet 验证集上实现了更好的速率精度性能，在相同 CR 下分类精度提高了 0.2% 到 1%，同时满足 HV 约束。 Deep Selector-JPEG也可以粗略地提供较高CR下的原始分类精度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种自适应JPEG图像压缩方法Deep Selector-JPEG，旨在在满足人类视觉标准的同时优化图像分类任务的压缩率与分类准确率的平衡。
AI.motivation: 传统JPEG压缩参数固定，难以在有限存储/带宽下兼顾计算机视觉任务的分类精度与人类视觉感知质量，需动态调整压缩参数以实现更优性能。
AI.method: 通过轻量或 heavyweight 选择器架构，为每张图像自适应选择质量因子(QF)，在满足预设MS-SSIM阈值（人类视觉标准）的前提下，优化压缩率与DNN分类准确率的权衡。
AI.result: 在相同压缩率下，Deep Selector-JPEG在ImageNet验证集上较传统JPEG提升分类准确率0.2%-1%，且在更高压缩率下仍可保持接近原始精度。
AI.conclusion: Deep Selector-JPEG通过自适应压缩策略有效提升了图像分类任务的率-精度性能，在满足人类视觉质量约束时优于传统JPEG方法。

title: Supervised Contrastive Learning and Feature Fusion for Improved Kinship Verification
title_zh: 用于改进亲属关系验证的监督对比学习和特征融合
abstract: Facial Kinship Verification is the task of determining the degree of familial relationship between two facial images. It has recently gained a lot of interest in various applications spanning forensic science, social media, and demographic studies. In the past decade, deep learning-based approaches have emerged as a promising solution to this problem, achieving state-of-the-art performance. In this paper, we propose a novel method for solving kinship verification by using supervised contrastive learning, which trains the model to maximize the similarity between related individuals and minimize it between unrelated individuals. Our experiments show state-of-the-art results and achieve 81.1% accuracy in the Families in the Wild (FIW) dataset.
summary: Facial Kinship Verification is the task of determining the degree of familial relationship between two facial images. It has recently gained a lot of interest in various applications spanning forensic science, social media, and demographic studies. In the past decade, deep learning-based approaches have emerged as a promising solution to this problem, achieving state-of-the-art performance. In this paper, we propose a novel method for solving kinship verification by using supervised contrastive learning, which trains the model to maximize the similarity between related individuals and minimize it between unrelated individuals. Our experiments show state-of-the-art results and achieve 81.1% accuracy in the Families in the Wild (FIW) dataset.
abstract_zh: 面部亲属关系验证是确定两个面部图像之间的家族关系程度的任务。最近，它在法医学、社交媒体和人口研究等各种应用中引起了人们的广泛兴趣。在过去的十年中，基于深度学习的方法已经成为解决这一问题的有希望的解决方案，并实现了最先进的性能。在本文中，我们提出了一种使用监督对比学习来解决亲属关系验证的新方法，该方法训练模型以最大化相关个体之间的相似性并最小化不相关个体之间的相似性。我们的实验展示了最先进的结果，在 Families in the Wild (FIW) 数据集中达到了 81.1% 的准确率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合监督对比学习和特征融合的方法，用于提升人脸识别中的亲属关系验证准确性。
AI.motivation: 尽管深度学习在亲属关系验证任务中取得进展，但现有方法在复杂场景下仍存在准确率不足的问题，需探索更有效的特征学习与融合策略。
AI.method: 采用监督对比学习框架，通过最大化亲属样本特征相似性、最小化非亲属样本相似性，并结合多模态特征融合技术增强表征能力。
AI.result: 在Families in the Wild数据集上达到81.1%的验证准确率，优于现有主流方法。
AI.conclusion: 监督对比学习与特征融合的结合有效提升了亲属关系验证性能，为相关应用提供了更可靠的解决方案。

title: Estimation and Early Prediction of Grip Force Based on sEMG Signals and Deep Recurrent Neural Networks
title_zh: 基于表面肌电信号和深度循环神经网络的握力估计和早期预测
comment: 9 pages, accepted for publication in journal of the Brazilian Society of Mechanical Sciences and Engineering
comments: 9 pages, accepted for publication in journal of the Brazilian Society of Mechanical Sciences and Engineering
abstract: Hands are used for communicating with the surrounding environment and have a complex structure that enables them to perform various tasks with their multiple degrees of freedom. Hand amputation can prevent a person from performing their daily activities. In that event, finding a suitable, fast, and reliable alternative for the missing limb can affect the lives of people who suffer from such conditions. As the most important use of the hands is to grasp objects, the purpose of this study is to accurately predict gripping force from surface electromyography (sEMG) signals during a pinch-type grip. In that regard, gripping force and sEMG signals are derived from 10 healthy subjects. Results show that for this task, recurrent networks outperform nonrecurrent ones, such as a fully connected multilayer perceptron (MLP) network. Gated recurrent unit (GRU) and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks can predict the gripping force with R-squared values of 0.994 and 0.992, respectively, and a prediction rate of over 1300 predictions per second. The predominant advantage of using such frameworks is that the gripping force can be predicted straight from preprocessed sEMG signals without any form of feature extraction, not to mention the ability to predict future force values using larger prediction horizons adequately. The methods presented in this study can be used in the myoelectric control of prosthetic hands or robotic grippers.

In that regard, gripping force and sEMG signals are derived from 10 healthy subjects. Results show that for this task, recurrent networks outperform nonrecurrent ones, such as a fully connected multilayer perceptron (MLP) network. Gated recurrent unit (GRU) and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks can predict the gripping force with R-squared values of 0.994 and 0.992, respectively, and a prediction rate of over 1300 predictions per second. The predominant advantage of using such frameworks is that the gripping force can be predicted straight from preprocessed sEMG signals without any form of feature extraction, not to mention the ability to predict future force values using larger prediction horizons adequately. The methods presented in this study can be used in the myoelectric control of prosthetic hands or robotic grippers.
abstract_zh: 手用于与周围环境进行交流，具有复杂的结构，使其能够以多个自由度执行各种任务。手部截肢可能会导致一个人无法进行日常活动。在这种情况下，为缺失的肢体找到合适、快速且可靠的替代方案可能会影响患有此类疾病的人的生活。由于手最重要的用途是抓握物体，因此本研究的目的是根据捏式抓握过程中的表面肌电 (sEMG) 信号准确预测抓握力。在这方面，握力和表面肌电信号来自 10 名健康受试者。结果表明，对于此任务，循环网络优于非循环网络，例如完全连接的多层感知器 (MLP) 网络。门控循环单元（GRU）和长短期记忆（LSTM）网络可以预测握力，R平方值分别为0.994和0.992，预测率超过每秒1300次。使用此类框架的主要优点是，可以直接从预处理的 sEMG 信号中预测握力，无需任何形式的特征提取，更不用说能够充分使用更大的预测范围来预测未来的力值。本研究中提出的方法可用于假手或机器人抓手的肌电控制。

AI.tldr: 本文基于表面肌电信号(sEMG)和深度循环神经网络，研究抓握力度的估计与早期预测方法。
AI.motivation: 手部截肢会影响日常活动，需寻找快速可靠的替代方案。抓握是手部最主要功能，准确预测抓握力度对假肢控制至关重要。
AI.method: 采用门控循环单元(GRU)和长短期记忆(LSTM)网络处理预处理后的sEMG信号，无需特征提取即可直接预测抓握力度。
AI.result: GRU和LSTM网络分别达到0.994和0.992的R平方值，预测速率达每秒1300次，且能有效预测未来力度值。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可应用于假肢手或机械夹爪的肌电控制，为截肢者提供高效精准的抓握力预测方案。

search_text: Estimation and Early Prediction of Grip Force Based on sEMG Signals and Deep Recurrent Neural Networks 基于表面肌电信号和深度循环神经网络的握力估计和早期预测 Hands are used for communicating with the surrounding environment and have a complex structure that enables them to perform various tasks with their multiple degrees of freedom. Hand amputation can prevent a person from performing their daily activities. In that event, finding a suitable, fast, and reliable alternative for the missing limb can affect the lives of people who suffer from such conditions. As the most important use of the hands is to grasp objects, the purpose of this study is to accurately predict gripping force from surface electromyography (sEMG) signals during a pinch-type grip. In that regard, gripping force and sEMG signals are derived from 10 healthy subjects. Results show that for this task, recurrent networks outperform nonrecurrent ones, such as a fully connected multilayer perceptron (MLP) network. Gated recurrent unit (GRU) and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks can predict the gripping force with R-squared values of 0.994 and 0.992, respectively, and a prediction rate of over 1300 predictions per second. The predominant advantage of using such frameworks is that the gripping force can be predicted straight from preprocessed sEMG signals without any form of feature extraction, not to mention the ability to predict future force values using larger prediction horizons adequately. The methods presented in this study can be used in the myoelectric control of prosthetic hands or robotic grippers. 手用于与周围环境进行交流，具有复杂的结构，使其能够以多个自由度执行各种任务。手部截肢可能会导致一个人无法进行日常活动。在这种情况下，为缺失的肢体找到合适、快速且可靠的替代方案可能会影响患有此类疾病的人的生活。由于手最重要的用途是抓握物体，因此本研究的目的是根据捏式抓握过程中的表面肌电 (sEMG) 信号准确预测抓握力。在这方面，握力和表面肌电信号来自 10 名健康受试者。结果表明，对于此任务，循环网络优于非循环网络，例如完全连接的多层感知器 (MLP) 网络。门控循环单元（GRU）和长短期记忆（LSTM）网络可以预测握力，R平方值分别为0.994和0.992，预测率超过每秒1300次。使用此类框架的主要优点是，可以直接从预处理的 sEMG 信号中预测握力，无需任何形式的特征提取，更不用说能够充分使用更大的预测范围来预测未来的力值。本研究中提出的方法可用于假手或机器人抓手的肌电控制。 本文基于表面肌电信号(sEMG)和深度循环神经网络，研究抓握力度的估计与早期预测方法。 Atusa Ghorbani,Aghil Yousefi-Koma,Amirhosein Vedadi cs.RO

title: Mixed Hierarchy Network for Image Restoration
title_zh: 用于图像恢复的混合层次网络
abstract: Image restoration is a long-standing low-level vision problem, e.g., deblurring and deraining. In the process of image restoration, it is necessary to consider not only the spatial details and contextual information of restoration to ensure the quality, but also the system complexity. Although many methods have been able to guarantee the quality of image restoration, the system complexity of the state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods is increasing as well. Motivated by this, we present a mixed hierarchy network that can balance these competing goals. Our main proposal is a mixed hierarchy architecture, that progressively recovers contextual information and spatial details from degraded images while we design intra-blocks to reduce system complexity. Specifically, our model first learns the contextual information using encoder-decoder architectures, and then combines them with high-resolution branches that preserve spatial detail. In order to reduce the system complexity of this architecture for convenient analysis and comparison, we replace or remove the nonlinear activation function with multiplication and use a simple network structure. In addition, we replace spatial convolution with global self-attention for the middle block of encoder-decoder. The resulting tightly interlinked hierarchy architecture, named as MHNet, delivers strong performance gains on several image restoration tasks, including image deraining, and deblurring.
summary: Image restoration is a long-standing low-level vision problem, e.g., deblurring and deraining. In the process of image restoration, it is necessary to consider not only the spatial details and contextual information of restoration to ensure the quality, but also the system complexity. Although many methods have been able to guarantee the quality of image restoration, the system complexity of the state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods is increasing as well. Motivated by this, we present a mixed hierarchy network that can balance these competing goals. Our main proposal is a mixed hierarchy architecture, that progressively recovers contextual information and spatial details from degraded images while we design intra-blocks to reduce system complexity. Specifically, our model first learns the contextual information using encoder-decoder architectures, and then combines them with high-resolution branches that preserve spatial detail. In order to reduce the system complexity of this architecture for convenient analysis and comparison, we replace or remove the nonlinear activation function with multiplication and use a simple network structure. In addition, we replace spatial convolution with global self-attention for the middle block of encoder-decoder. The resulting tightly interlinked hierarchy architecture, named as MHNet, delivers strong performance gains on several image restoration tasks, including image deraining, and deblurring.
abstract_zh: 图像恢复是一个长期存在的低级视觉问题，例如去模糊和去雨。在图像复原过程中，不仅要考虑复原的空间细节和上下文信息以保证质量，还要考虑系统的复杂度。尽管许多方法已经能够保证图像恢复的质量，但最先进（SOTA）方法的系统复杂性也在增加。受此启发，我们提出了一个混合层次网络，可以平衡这些相互竞争的目标。我们的主要建议是混合层次结构，它逐步从退化图像中恢复上下文信息和空间细节，同时我们设计块内以降低系统复杂性。具体来说，我们的模型首先使用编码器-解码器架构学习上下文信息，然后将它们与保留空间细节的高分辨率分支相结合。为了降低该架构的系统复杂度以便于分析和比较，我们用乘法替换或去除非线性激活函数并使用简单的网络结构。此外，我们将编码器-解码器中间块的空间卷积替换为全局自注意力。由此产生的紧密互连的层次结构（称为 MHNet）在多项图像恢复任务（包括图像去雨和去模糊）中提供了强大的性能提升。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种混合分层网络（MHNet），用于在图像修复任务中平衡系统复杂度与修复质量。
AI.motivation: 现有图像修复方法虽能保证质量，但系统复杂度持续增加，需在保持质量的同时降低复杂度。
AI.method: 设计混合分层架构，结合编码器-解码器提取上下文信息与高分辨率分支保留空间细节，通过乘法替代非线性激活、简化网络结构并采用全局自注意力机制降低复杂度。
AI.result: MHNet在图像去雨、去模糊等任务中取得显著性能提升，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 混合分层架构成功平衡了图像修复的精度与系统复杂度，为低层次视觉任务提供新思路。

title: Topological Feature Selection
title_zh: 拓扑特征选择
comment: Accepted at the 2nd Annual Workshop on Topology, Algebra, and Geometry in Machine Learning (TAG-ML) at the 40th International Conference on Machine Learning, Honolulu, Hawaii, USA. 2023. 23 pages, 2 figures, 13 tables
comments: Accepted at the 2nd Annual Workshop on Topology, Algebra, and Geometry in Machine Learning (TAG-ML) at the 40th International Conference on Machine Learning, Honolulu, Hawaii, USA. 2023. 23 pages, 2 figures, 13 tables
abstract: In this paper, we introduce a novel unsupervised, graph-based filter feature selection technique which exploits the power of topologically constrained network representations. We model dependency structures among features using a family of chordal graphs (the Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph), and we maximise the likelihood of features' relevance by studying their relative position inside the network. Such an approach presents three aspects that are particularly satisfactory compared to its alternatives: (i) it is highly tunable and easily adaptable to the nature of input data; (ii) it is fully explainable, maintaining, at the same time, a remarkable level of simplicity; (iii) it is computationally cheaper compared to its alternatives. We test our algorithm on 16 benchmark datasets from different applicative domains showing that it outperforms or matches the current state-of-the-art under heterogeneous evaluation conditions.
summary: In this paper, we introduce a novel unsupervised, graph-based filter feature selection technique which exploits the power of topologically constrained network representations. We model dependency structures among features using a family of chordal graphs (the Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph), and we maximise the likelihood of features' relevance by studying their relative position inside the network. Such an approach presents three aspects that are particularly satisfactory compared to its alternatives: (i) it is highly tunable and easily adaptable to the nature of input data; (ii) it is fully explainable, maintaining, at the same time, a remarkable level of simplicity; (iii) it is computationally cheaper compared to its alternatives. We test our algorithm on 16 benchmark datasets from different applicative domains showing that it outperforms or matches the current state-of-the-art under heterogeneous evaluation conditions.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的无监督、基于图的过滤器特征选择技术，该技术利用了拓扑约束网络表示的力量。我们使用一系列弦图（三角最大过滤图）对特征之间的依赖结构进行建模，并通过研究特征在网络内的相对位置来最大化特征相关性的可能性。与其他方法相比，这种方法具有特别令人满意的三个方面：（i）它具有高度可调性并且易于适应输入数据的性质； (ii) 它是完全可解释的，同时保持了显着的简单性； (iii) 与其替代方案相比，它的计算成本更低。我们在来自不同应用领域的 16 个基准数据集上测试了我们的算法，结果表明它在异构评估条件下优于或匹配当前最先进的技术。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于图的无监督特征选择方法，利用拓扑约束网络表示优化特征相关性。
AI.motivation: 现有特征选择方法存在计算复杂度高、可解释性差或适应性不足的问题，需更高效且可解释的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过弦图建模特征依赖结构，利用Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph优化特征在网络中的相对位置以最大化相关性。
AI.result: 在16个基准数据集上测试显示，该方法在异构评估条件下优于或匹配当前最先进的特征选择技术。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法在可调性、可解释性和计算效率上优于现有方法，具有广泛的应用潜力。

motivation: 现有特征选择方法存在计算复杂度高、可解释性差或适应性不足的问题，需更高效且可解释的解决方案。
method: 通过弦图建模特征依赖结构，利用Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph优化特征在网络中的相对位置以最大化相关性。

search_text: Topological Feature Selection 拓扑特征选择 In this paper, we introduce a novel unsupervised, graph-based filter feature selection technique which exploits the power of topologically constrained network representations. We model dependency structures among features using a family of chordal graphs (the Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graph), and we maximise the likelihood of features' relevance by studying their relative position inside the network. Such an approach presents three aspects that are particularly satisfactory compared to its alternatives: (i) it is highly tunable and easily adaptable to the nature of input data; (ii) it is fully explainable, maintaining, at the same time, a remarkable level of simplicity; (iii) it is computationally cheaper compared to its alternatives. We test our algorithm on 16 benchmark datasets from different applicative domains showing that it outperforms or matches the current state-of-the-art under heterogeneous evaluation conditions. 在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的无监督、基于图的过滤器特征选择技术，该技术利用了拓扑约束网络表示的力量。我们使用一系列弦图（三角最大过滤图）对特征之间的依赖结构进行建模，并通过研究特征在网络内的相对位置来最大化特征相关性的可能性。与其他方法相比，这种方法具有特别令人满意的三个方面：（i）它具有高度可调性并且易于适应输入数据的性质； (ii) 它是完全可解释的，同时保持了显着的简单性； (iii) 与其替代方案相比，它的计算成本更低。我们在来自不同应用领域的 16 个基准数据集上测试了我们的算法，结果表明它在异构评估条件下优于或匹配当前最先进的技术。 本文提出一种基于图的无监督特征选择方法，利用拓扑约束网络表示优化特征相关性。 Antonio Briola,Tomaso Aste cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Weigang Lu,Ziyu Guan,Wei Zhao,Yaming Yang,Yuanhai Lv,Lining Xing,Baosheng Yu,Dacheng Tao
title: Pseudo Contrastive Learning for Graph-based Semi-supervised Learning
title_zh: 基于图的半监督学习的伪对比学习
abstract: Pseudo Labeling is a technique used to improve the performance of semi-supervised Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) by generating additional pseudo-labels based on confident predictions. However, the quality of generated pseudo-labels has been a longstanding concern due to the sensitivity of the classification objective with respect to the given labels. To avoid the untrustworthy classification supervision indicating ``a node belongs to a specific class,'' we favor the fault-tolerant contrasting supervision demonstrating ``two nodes do not belong to the same class.'' Thus, the problem of generating high-quality pseudo-labels is then transformed into a relaxed version, i.e., identifying reliable negative pairs. To achieve this, we propose a general framework for GNNs, termed Pseudo Contrastive Learning (PCL). It separates two nodes whose positive and negative pseudo-labels target the same class. To incorporate topological knowledge into learning, we devise a topologically weighted contrastive loss that spends more effort separating negative pairs with smaller topological distances. Experimentally, we apply PCL to various GNNs, which consistently outperform their counterparts using other popular general techniques on five real-world graphs.
summary: Pseudo Labeling is a technique used to improve the performance of semi-supervised Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) by generating additional pseudo-labels based on confident predictions. However, the quality of generated pseudo-labels has been a longstanding concern due to the sensitivity of the classification objective with respect to the given labels. To avoid the untrustworthy classification supervision indicating ``a node belongs to a specific class,'' we favor the fault-tolerant contrasting supervision demonstrating ``two nodes do not belong to the same class.'' Thus, the problem of generating high-quality pseudo-labels is then transformed into a relaxed version, i.e., identifying reliable negative pairs. To achieve this, we propose a general framework for GNNs, termed Pseudo Contrastive Learning (PCL). It separates two nodes whose positive and negative pseudo-labels target the same class. To incorporate topological knowledge into learning, we devise a topologically weighted contrastive loss that spends more effort separating negative pairs with smaller topological distances. Experimentally, we apply PCL to various GNNs, which consistently outperform their counterparts using other popular general techniques on five real-world graphs.
abstract_zh: 伪标签是一种通过基于置信预测生成附加伪标签来提高半监督图神经网络 (GNN) 性能的技术。然而，由于分类目标相对于给定标签的敏感性，生成的伪标签的质量一直是一个长期关注的问题。为了避免表明“一个节点属于特定类”的不可信分类监督，我们支持证明“两个节点不属于同一类”的容错对比监督。因此，生成高质量伪标签的问题被转化为一个宽松的版本，即识别可靠的负对。为了实现这一目标，我们提出了一个 GNN 的通用框架，称为伪对比学习（PCL）。它将正负伪标签针对同一类的两个节点分开。为了将拓扑知识融入学习中，我们设计了一种拓扑加权对比损失，它花费更多的精力来分离拓扑距离较小的负对。实验上，我们将 PCL 应用于各种 GNN，这些 GNN 在五个真实世界图上始终优于使用其他流行通用技术的对应模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于伪对比学习的图半监督学习框架，通过生成可靠负样本对提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 传统伪标签方法因分类目标对标签敏感而存在质量隐患，作者认为对比学习中'非同类'的负样本监督更具鲁棒性。
AI.method: 设计Pseudo Contrastive Learning框架，通过分离正负伪标签节点并引入拓扑权重对比损失，强化对小拓扑距离负样本对的区分能力。
AI.result: 在五个真实图数据集上，PCL方法在多种GNN模型中均优于其他主流半监督学习技术。
AI.conclusion: 通过将伪标签生成转化为负样本对识别问题，并结合拓扑结构优化对比学习，有效提升了图半监督学习的性能。

title: A Comprehensive Evaluation Study on Risk Level Classification of Melanoma by Computer Vision on ISIC 2016-2020 Datasets
title_zh: ISIC 2016-2020数据集上计算机视觉对黑色素瘤风险等级分类的综合评价研究
abstract: Skin cancer is the most common type of cancer. Specifically, melanoma is the cause of 75% of skin cancer deaths, although it is the least common skin cancer. Better detection of melanoma could have a positive impact on millions of people. The ISIC archive contains the largest publicly available collection of dermatoscopic images of skin lesions. In this research, we investigate the efficacy of applying advanced deep learning techniques in computer vision to identify melanoma in images of skin lesions. Through reviewing previous methods, including pre-trained models, deep-learning classifiers, transfer learning, etc., we demonstrate the applicability of the popular deep learning methods on critical clinical problems such as identifying melanoma. Finally, we proposed a processing flow with a validation AUC greater than 94% and a sensitivity greater than 90% on ISIC 2016 - 2020 datasets.
summary: Skin cancer is the most common type of cancer. Specifically, melanoma is the cause of 75% of skin cancer deaths, although it is the least common skin cancer. Better detection of melanoma could have a positive impact on millions of people. The ISIC archive contains the largest publicly available collection of dermatoscopic images of skin lesions. In this research, we investigate the efficacy of applying advanced deep learning techniques in computer vision to identify melanoma in images of skin lesions. Through reviewing previous methods, including pre-trained models, deep-learning classifiers, transfer learning, etc., we demonstrate the applicability of the popular deep learning methods on critical clinical problems such as identifying melanoma. Finally, we proposed a processing flow with a validation AUC greater than 94% and a sensitivity greater than 90% on ISIC 2016 - 2020 datasets.
abstract_zh: 皮肤癌是最常见的癌症类型。具体来说，黑色素瘤是 75% 皮肤癌死亡的原因，尽管它是最不常见的皮肤癌。更好地检测黑色素瘤可能会对数百万人产生积极影响。 ISIC 档案包含最大的公开可用的皮肤病变皮肤镜图像集。在这项研究中，我们研究了在计算机视觉中应用先进的深度学习技术来识别皮肤病变图像中黑色素瘤的功效。通过回顾以前的方法，包括预训练模型、深度学习分类器、迁移学习等，我们证明了流行的深度学习方法在识别黑色素瘤等关键临床问题上的适用性。最后，我们提出了一个处理流程，在 ISIC 2016 - 2020 数据集上验证 AUC 大于 94%，灵敏度大于 90%。

AI.tldr: 该论文通过计算机视觉技术对ISIC 2016-2020数据集中的黑色素瘤进行风险等级分类评估。
AI.motivation: 黑色素瘤虽罕见但导致75%的皮肤癌死亡，早期检测对挽救生命至关重要，而ISIC数据集提供了大量皮肤病变图像用于研究。
AI.method: 综述预训练模型、深度学习分类器及迁移学习等方法，提出结合深度学习的图像处理流程。
AI.result: 在ISIC 2016-2020数据集上实现验证AUC超过94%、敏感度超过90%的分类效果。
AI.conclusion: 先进深度学习方法在黑色素瘤检测等临床问题中具有显著应用潜力。

authors_text: Alexey Sholokhov,Nikita Kuzmin,Kong Aik Lee,Eng Siong Chng
title: Probabilistic Back-ends for Online Speaker Recognition and Clustering
title_zh: 用于在线说话人识别和聚类的概率后端
abstract: This paper focuses on multi-enrollment speaker recognition which naturally occurs in the task of online speaker clustering, and studies the properties of different scoring back-ends in this scenario. First, we show that popular cosine scoring suffers from poor score calibration with a varying number of enrollment utterances. Second, we propose a simple replacement for cosine scoring based on an extremely constrained version of probabilistic linear discriminant analysis (PLDA). The proposed model improves over the cosine scoring for multi-enrollment recognition while keeping the same performance in the case of one-to-one comparisons. Finally, we consider an online speaker clustering task where each step naturally involves multi-enrollment recognition. We propose an online clustering algorithm allowing us to take benefits from the PLDA model such as the ability to handle uncertainty and better score calibration. Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.
summary: This paper focuses on multi-enrollment speaker recognition which naturally occurs in the task of online speaker clustering, and studies the properties of different scoring back-ends in this scenario. First, we show that popular cosine scoring suffers from poor score calibration with a varying number of enrollment utterances. Second, we propose a simple replacement for cosine scoring based on an extremely constrained version of probabilistic linear discriminant analysis (PLDA). The proposed model improves over the cosine scoring for multi-enrollment recognition while keeping the same performance in the case of one-to-one comparisons. Finally, we consider an online speaker clustering task where each step naturally involves multi-enrollment recognition. We propose an online clustering algorithm allowing us to take benefits from the PLDA model such as the ability to handle uncertainty and better score calibration. Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.
abstract_zh: 本文重点关注在线说话人聚类任务中自然发生的多注册说话人识别，并研究该场景中不同评分后端的特性。首先，我们表明，流行的余弦评分因注册话语数量不同而导致分数校准不佳。其次，我们提出了基于概率线性判别分析（PLDA）的极其受限版本的余弦评分的简单替代。所提出的模型改进了多注册识别的余弦评分，同时在一对一比较的情况下保持相同的性能。最后，我们考虑一个在线说话人聚类任务，其中每个步骤自然涉及多注册识别。我们提出了一种在线聚类算法，使我们能够从 PLDA 模型中受益，例如处理不确定性和更好的分数校准的能力。我们的实验证明了所提出算法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究在线说话人识别与聚类中的概率后端方法，提出基于PLDA模型的改进方案以解决多注册场景下的评分校准问题。
AI.motivation: 传统余弦评分在多注册语句场景下存在校准不足的问题，影响说话人识别与聚类的准确性，需寻找更稳健的评分后端方法。
AI.method: 提出极简PLDA模型替代余弦评分，设计在线聚类算法以利用PLDA的不确定性处理能力和更优校准特性。
AI.result: 实验表明PLDA方法在多注册识别中优于余弦评分且保持一对一性能，所提在线聚类算法有效提升系统表现。
AI.conclusion: 基于PLDA的概率后端及在线聚类算法能有效解决多注册场景下的评分校准问题，为在线说话人系统提供更可靠方案。

search_text: Probabilistic Back-ends for Online Speaker Recognition and Clustering 用于在线说话人识别和聚类的概率后端 This paper focuses on multi-enrollment speaker recognition which naturally occurs in the task of online speaker clustering, and studies the properties of different scoring back-ends in this scenario. First, we show that popular cosine scoring suffers from poor score calibration with a varying number of enrollment utterances. Second, we propose a simple replacement for cosine scoring based on an extremely constrained version of probabilistic linear discriminant analysis (PLDA). The proposed model improves over the cosine scoring for multi-enrollment recognition while keeping the same performance in the case of one-to-one comparisons. Finally, we consider an online speaker clustering task where each step naturally involves multi-enrollment recognition. We propose an online clustering algorithm allowing us to take benefits from the PLDA model such as the ability to handle uncertainty and better score calibration. Our experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm. 本文重点关注在线说话人聚类任务中自然发生的多注册说话人识别，并研究该场景中不同评分后端的特性。首先，我们表明，流行的余弦评分因注册话语数量不同而导致分数校准不佳。其次，我们提出了基于概率线性判别分析（PLDA）的极其受限版本的余弦评分的简单替代。所提出的模型改进了多注册识别的余弦评分，同时在一对一比较的情况下保持相同的性能。最后，我们考虑一个在线说话人聚类任务，其中每个步骤自然涉及多注册识别。我们提出了一种在线聚类算法，使我们能够从 PLDA 模型中受益，例如处理不确定性和更好的分数校准的能力。我们的实验证明了所提出算法的有效性。 本文研究在线说话人识别与聚类中的概率后端方法，提出基于PLDA模型的改进方案以解决多注册场景下的评分校准问题。 Alexey Sholokhov,Nikita Kuzmin,Kong Aik Lee,Eng Siong Chng eess.AS,cs.LG,cs.SD,eess.SP

title: Interactive Video Corpus Moment Retrieval using Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 使用强化学习的交互式视频语料库时刻检索
abstract: Known-item video search is effective with human-in-the-loop to interactively investigate the search result and refine the initial query. Nevertheless, when the first few pages of results are swamped with visually similar items, or the search target is hidden deep in the ranked list, finding the know-item target usually requires a long duration of browsing and result inspection. This paper tackles the problem by reinforcement learning, aiming to reach a search target within a few rounds of interaction by long-term learning from user feedbacks. Specifically, the system interactively plans for navigation path based on feedback and recommends a potential target that maximizes the long-term reward for user comment. We conduct experiments for the challenging task of video corpus moment retrieval (VCMR) to localize moments from a large video corpus. The experimental results on TVR and DiDeMo datasets verify that our proposed work is effective in retrieving the moments that are hidden deep inside the ranked lists of CONQUER and HERO, which are the state-of-the-art auto-search engines for VCMR.
summary: Known-item video search is effective with human-in-the-loop to interactively investigate the search result and refine the initial query. Nevertheless, when the first few pages of results are swamped with visually similar items, or the search target is hidden deep in the ranked list, finding the know-item target usually requires a long duration of browsing and result inspection. This paper tackles the problem by reinforcement learning, aiming to reach a search target within a few rounds of interaction by long-term learning from user feedbacks. Specifically, the system interactively plans for navigation path based on feedback and recommends a potential target that maximizes the long-term reward for user comment. We conduct experiments for the challenging task of video corpus moment retrieval (VCMR) to localize moments from a large video corpus. The experimental results on TVR and DiDeMo datasets verify that our proposed work is effective in retrieving the moments that are hidden deep inside the ranked lists of CONQUER and HERO, which are the state-of-the-art auto-search engines for VCMR.
abstract_zh: 已知项目视频搜索可以有效地利用人机交互方式调查搜索结果并细化初始查询。然而，当结果的前几页充斥着视觉上相似的项目，或者搜索目标隐藏在排名列表深处时，找到已知项目目标通常需要长时间的浏览和结果检查。本文通过强化学习来解决这个问题，旨在通过对用户反馈的长期学习，在几轮交互内达到搜索目标。具体来说，系统根据反馈交互式地规划导航路径，并推荐一个潜在目标，最大限度地提高用户评论的长期奖励。我们针对视频语料库时刻检索（VCMR）这一具有挑战性的任务进行了实验，以定位大型视频语料库中的时刻。 TVR 和 DiDeMo 数据集上的实验结果验证了我们提出的工作可以有效地检索隐藏在 CONQUER 和 HERO 排名列表深处的时刻，这是最先进的 VCMR 自动搜索引擎。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于强化学习的交互式视频语料库时刻检索方法，解决传统搜索在深层结果检索效率低的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有视频搜索在目标被视觉相似项淹没或位于排名列表深处时，需用户长时间浏览才能定位目标，效率低下。
AI.method: 通过强化学习建模用户反馈，交互式规划导航路径并推荐能最大化长期奖励的潜在目标，优化检索过程。
AI.result: 在TVR和DiDeMo数据集上验证，该方法有效检索出CONQUER和HERO等先进系统排名列表深处的隐藏目标。
AI.conclusion: 强化学习框架显著提升了深层视频时刻检索的效率，为交互式视频搜索提供了新思路。

authors_text: Atif Shah,Maged S. Al-Shaibani,Moataz Ahmad,Reem Bunyan
title: A Bibliography of Multiple Sclerosis Lesions Detection Methods using Brain MRIs
title_zh: 使用脑 MRI 检测多发性硬化症病变方法的参考书目
abstract: Introduction: Multiple Sclerosis (MS) is a chronic disease that affects millions of people across the globe. MS can critically affect different organs of the central nervous system such as the eyes, the spinal cord, and the brain. Background: To help physicians in diagnosing MS lesions, computer-aided methods are widely used. In this regard, a considerable research has been carried out in the area of automatic detection and segmentation of MS lesions in magnetic resonance images (MRIs). Methodology: In this study, we review the different approaches that have been used in computer-aided detection and segmentation of MS lesions. Our review resulted in categorizing MS lesion segmentation approaches into six broad categories: data-driven, statistical, supervised machine learning, unsupervised machine learning, fuzzy, and deep learning-based techniques. We critically analyze the different techniques under these approaches and highlight their strengths and weaknesses. Results: From the study, we observe that a considerable amount of work, around 25% of related literature, is focused on statistical-based MS lesion segmentation techniques, followed by 21.15% for data-driven based methods, 19.23% for deep learning and 15.38% for supervised methods. Implication: The study points out the challenges/gaps to be addressed in future research. The study shows the work which has been done in last one decade in detection and segmentation of MS lesions. The results show that, in recent years, deep learning methods are outperforming all the others methods.

abstract_zh: 简介：多发性硬化症 (MS) 是一种影响全球数百万人的慢性疾病。多发性硬化症会严重影响中枢神经系统的不同器官，例如眼睛、脊髓和大脑。背景：为了帮助医生诊断多发性硬化症病变，计算机辅助方法被广泛使用。在这方面，在磁共振图像（MRI）中多发性硬化症病变的自动检测和分割领域已经进行了大量的研究。方法：在本研究中，我们回顾了计算机辅助检测和多发性硬化症病变分割中使用的不同方法。我们的审查结果将多发性硬化症病变分割方法分为六大类：数据驱动、统计、监督机器学习、无监督机器学习、模糊和基于深度学习的技术。我们批判性地分析这些方法下的不同技术，并强调它们的优点和缺点。结果：从研究中，我们观察到相当多的工作（大约 25% 的相关文献）集中在基于统计的 MS 病灶分割技术，其次是基于数据驱动的方法，占 21.15%，深度学习占 19.23%，监督方法占 15.38%。含义：该研究指出了未来研究中需要解决的挑战/差距。该研究展示了过去十年在多发性硬化症病变检测和分割方面所做的工作。结果表明，近年来，深度学习方法的性能优于所有其他方法。

AI.tldr: 该论文综述了用于脑部磁共振成像（MRI）中多发性硬化症（MS）病灶检测与分割的计算机辅助方法，并将其分类为六种主要技术类别。
AI.motivation: 通过系统梳理现有方法的优势与不足，为医学诊断提供技术参考，并指出未来研究方向以解决当前技术挑战。
AI.method: 对相关文献进行系统性回顾，将MS病灶分割方法划分为数据驱动、统计学、监督学习、无监督学习、模糊方法及深度学习六大类，并进行批判性分析。
AI.result: 统计方法占25%，数据驱动方法占21.15%，深度学习占19.23%，监督方法占15.38%；近年深度学习方法表现最优。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习虽表现突出，但现有方法仍存在局限性，未来需进一步优化算法以提高检测精度与临床适用性。
