authors_text: Bo Zhou,Jo Schlemper,Neel Dey,Seyed Sadegh Mohseni Salehi,Kevin Sheth,Chi Liu,James S. Duncan,Michal Sofka
title: Dual-Domain Self-Supervised Learning for Accelerated Non-Cartesian MRI Reconstruction
title_zh: 用于加速非笛卡尔 MRI 重建的双域自监督学习
comment: 14 pages, 10 figures, published at Medical Image Analysis (MedIA)
comments: 14 pages, 10 figures, published at Medical Image Analysis (MedIA)
abstract: While enabling accelerated acquisition and improved reconstruction accuracy, current deep MRI reconstruction networks are typically supervised, require fully sampled data, and are limited to Cartesian sampling patterns. These factors limit their practical adoption as fully-sampled MRI is prohibitively time-consuming to acquire clinically. Further, non-Cartesian sampling patterns are particularly desirable as they are more amenable to acceleration and show improved motion robustness. To this end, we present a fully self-supervised approach for accelerated non-Cartesian MRI reconstruction which leverages self-supervision in both k-space and image domains. In training, the undersampled data are split into disjoint k-space domain partitions. For the k-space self-supervision, we train a network to reconstruct the input undersampled data from both the disjoint partitions and from itself. For the image-level self-supervision, we enforce appearance consistency obtained from the original undersampled data and the two partitions. Experimental results on our simulated multi-coil non-Cartesian MRI dataset demonstrate that DDSS can generate high-quality reconstruction that approaches the accuracy of the fully supervised reconstruction, outperforming previous baseline methods. Finally, DDSS is shown to scale to highly challenging real-world clinical MRI reconstruction acquired on a portable low-field (0.064 T) MRI scanner with no data available for supervised training while demonstrating improved image quality as compared to traditional reconstruction, as determined by a radiologist study.
summary: While enabling accelerated acquisition and improved reconstruction accuracy, current deep MRI reconstruction networks are typically supervised, require fully sampled data, and are limited to Cartesian sampling patterns. These factors limit their practical adoption as fully-sampled MRI is prohibitively time-consuming to acquire clinically. Further, non-Cartesian sampling patterns are particularly desirable as they are more amenable to acceleration and show improved motion robustness. To this end, we present a fully self-supervised approach for accelerated non-Cartesian MRI reconstruction which leverages self-supervision in both k-space and image domains. In training, the undersampled data are split into disjoint k-space domain partitions. For the k-space self-supervision, we train a network to reconstruct the input undersampled data from both the disjoint partitions and from itself. For the image-level self-supervision, we enforce appearance consistency obtained from the original undersampled data and the two partitions. Experimental results on our simulated multi-coil non-Cartesian MRI dataset demonstrate that DDSS can generate high-quality reconstruction that approaches the accuracy of the fully supervised reconstruction, outperforming previous baseline methods. Finally, DDSS is shown to scale to highly challenging real-world clinical MRI reconstruction acquired on a portable low-field (0.064 T) MRI scanner with no data available for supervised training while demonstrating improved image quality as compared to traditional reconstruction, as determined by a radiologist study.
abstract_zh: 在实现加速采集和提高重建精度的同时，当前的深度 MRI 重建网络通常受到监督，需要完全采样的数据，并且仅限于笛卡尔采样模式。这些因素限制了它们的实际应用，因为在临床上获取完全采样的 MRI 非常耗时。此外，非笛卡尔采样模式是特别理想的，因为它们更适合加速并且显示出改进的运动鲁棒性。为此，我们提出了一种完全自我监督的方法，用于加速非笛卡尔 MRI 重建，该方法利用 k 空间和图像域中的自我监督。在训练中，欠采样数据被分成不相交的 k 空间域分区。对于 k 空间自监督，我们训练一个网络来从不相交的分区和自身重建输入欠采样数据。对于图像级自监督，我们强制从原始欠采样数据和两个分区获得外观一致性。我们模拟的多线圈非笛卡尔 MRI 数据集的实验结果表明，DDSS 可以生成接近完全监督重建精度的高质量重建，优于以前的基线方法。最后，DDSS 被证明可以扩展到在便携式低场 (0.064 T) MRI 扫描仪上获得的极具挑战性的现实世界临床 MRI 重建，没有可用于监督训练的数据，同时根据放射科医生的研究确定，与传统重建相比，图像质量得到了改善。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种双域自监督学习方法DDSS，用于加速非笛卡尔MRI重建，无需依赖完全采样数据。
AI.motivation: 现有MRI重建网络需完全采样数据且仅适用于笛卡尔采样，而临床中完全采样耗时且非笛卡尔采样更优但缺乏有效重建方法。
AI.method: 通过k空间和图像域双重自监督：训练时分割k空间数据，利用不相交分区与输入数据互为监督；图像域强制原始数据与分区重建结果的外观一致性。
AI.result: 在模拟多线圈非笛卡尔数据集上，DDSS重建质量接近全监督方法且优于基线；在0.064T便携式MRI临床数据中显著提升图像质量（放射科医生评估）。
AI.conclusion: DDSS无需监督数据即可实现高质量非笛卡尔MRI重建，适用于临床实际场景特别是低场MRI设备。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种双域自监督学习方法DDSS，用于加速非笛卡尔MRI重建，无需依赖完全采样数据。

title: A Federated Approach for Hate Speech Detection
title_zh: 仇恨言论检测的联合方法
abstract: Hate speech detection has been the subject of high research attention, due to the scale of content created on social media. In spite of the attention and the sensitive nature of the task, privacy preservation in hate speech detection has remained under-studied. The majority of research has focused on centralised machine learning infrastructures which risk leaking data. In this paper, we show that using federated machine learning can help address privacy the concerns that are inherent to hate speech detection while obtaining up to 6.81% improvement in terms of F1-score.
summary: Hate speech detection has been the subject of high research attention, due to the scale of content created on social media. In spite of the attention and the sensitive nature of the task, privacy preservation in hate speech detection has remained under-studied. The majority of research has focused on centralised machine learning infrastructures which risk leaking data. In this paper, we show that using federated machine learning can help address privacy the concerns that are inherent to hate speech detection while obtaining up to 6.81% improvement in terms of F1-score.
abstract_zh: 由于社交媒体上创建的内容规模巨大，仇恨言论检测一直是研究高度关注的主题。尽管这项任务受到关注并且具有敏感性，但仇恨言论检测中的隐私保护仍然没有得到充分研究。大多数研究都集中在存在数据泄露风险的集中式机器学习基础设施上。在本文中，我们表明，使用联合机器学习可以帮助解决仇恨语音检测固有的隐私问题，同时在 F1 分数方面获得高达 6.81% 的改进。

AI.tldr: 提出一种联邦学习方法用于仇恨言论检测，以解决隐私问题并提升检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有仇恨言论检测多采用中心化机器学习，存在数据泄露风险，且隐私保护研究不足，需探索更安全的解决方案。
AI.method: 采用联邦机器学习框架，在分布式设备上训练模型，无需集中用户数据，从而保护隐私。
AI.result: 相比传统方法，在F1分数上提升了6.81%，同时有效保护用户隐私。
AI.conclusion: 联邦学习能有效平衡隐私保护与检测性能，为仇恨言论检测提供更安全的解决方案。

title: Faster Wavelet Tree Queries
title_zh: 更快的小波树查询
abstract: Given a text, rank and select queries return the number of occurrences of a character up to a position (rank) or the position of a character with a given rank (select). These queries have applications in, e.g., compression, computational geometry, and most notably pattern matching in the form of the backward search -- the backbone of many compressed full-text indices. Currently, in practice, for text over non-binary alphabets, the wavelet tree is probably the most used data structure for rank and select queries. In this paper, we present techniques to speed up queries by a factor of two (access and select) up to three (rank), compared to the wavelet tree implementation contained in the widely used Succinct Data Structure Library (SDSL). To this end, we change the underlying tree structure from a binary tree to a 4-ary tree and reduce cache misses by approximating rank queries using a predictive model to prefetch all data required for the actual rank query.
summary: Given a text, rank and select queries return the number of occurrences of a character up to a position (rank) or the position of a character with a given rank (select). These queries have applications in, e.g., compression, computational geometry, and most notably pattern matching in the form of the backward search -- the backbone of many compressed full-text indices. Currently, in practice, for text over non-binary alphabets, the wavelet tree is probably the most used data structure for rank and select queries. In this paper, we present techniques to speed up queries by a factor of two (access and select) up to three (rank), compared to the wavelet tree implementation contained in the widely used Succinct Data Structure Library (SDSL). To this end, we change the underlying tree structure from a binary tree to a 4-ary tree and reduce cache misses by approximating rank queries using a predictive model to prefetch all data required for the actual rank query.
abstract_zh: 给定文本，排名和选择查询返回字符在某个位置（排名）或具有给定排名（选择）的字符的位置的出现次数。这些查询在压缩、计算几何等方面有应用，最值得注意的是向后搜索形式的模式匹配——许多压缩全文索引的支柱。目前，在实践中，对于非二进制字母表上的文本，小波树可能是排序和选择查询最常用的数据结构。在本文中，与广泛使用的简洁数据结构库（SDSL）中包含的小波树实现相比，我们提出了将查询速度提高两倍（访问和选择）到三倍（排名）的技术。为此，我们将底层树结构从二叉树更改为四叉树，并通过使用预测模型预取实际排名查询所需的所有数据来近似排名查询，从而减少缓存未命中。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过改进波纹树结构和引入预测模型来加速rank和select查询。
AI.motivation: 现有波纹树在非二进制文本处理中存在查询效率瓶颈，需优化以满足压缩索引等应用需求。
AI.method: 将二叉树结构改为四叉树，并利用预测模型预取数据减少缓存未命中。
AI.result: 相比SDSL库实现，access和select查询加速2倍，rank查询加速3倍。
AI.conclusion: 四叉树结构与预测模型结合有效提升波纹树查询性能，适用于需要高效rank/select操作的场景。

title: KLIF: An optimized spiking neuron unit for tuning surrogate gradient slope and membrane potential
title_zh: KLIF：一种优化的尖峰神经元单元，用于调整替代梯度斜率和膜电位
abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) have attracted much attention due to their ability to process temporal information, low power consumption, and higher biological plausibility. However, it is still challenging to develop efficient and high-performing learning algorithms for SNNs. Methods like artificial neural network (ANN)-to-SNN conversion can transform ANNs to SNNs with slight performance loss, but it needs a long simulation to approximate the rate coding. Directly training SNN by spike-based backpropagation (BP) such as surrogate gradient approximation is more flexible. Yet now, the performance of SNNs is not competitive compared with ANNs. In this paper, we propose a novel k-based leaky Integrate-and-Fire (KLIF) neuron model to improve the learning ability of SNNs. Compared with the popular leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF) model, KLIF adds a learnable scaling factor to dynamically update the slope and width of the surrogate gradient curve during training and incorporates a ReLU activation function that selectively delivers membrane potential to spike firing and resetting. The proposed spiking unit is evaluated on both static MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10 datasets, as well as neuromorphic N-MNIST, CIFAR10-DVS, and DVS128-Gesture datasets. Experiments indicate that KLIF performs much better than LIF without introducing additional computational cost and achieves state-of-the-art performance on these datasets with few time steps. Also, KLIF is believed to be more biological plausible than LIF. The good performance of KLIF can make it completely replace the role of LIF in SNN for various tasks.
summary: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) have attracted much attention due to their ability to process temporal information, low power consumption, and higher biological plausibility. However, it is still challenging to develop efficient and high-performing learning algorithms for SNNs. Methods like artificial neural network (ANN)-to-SNN conversion can transform ANNs to SNNs with slight performance loss, but it needs a long simulation to approximate the rate coding. Directly training SNN by spike-based backpropagation (BP) such as surrogate gradient approximation is more flexible. Yet now, the performance of SNNs is not competitive compared with ANNs. In this paper, we propose a novel k-based leaky Integrate-and-Fire (KLIF) neuron model to improve the learning ability of SNNs. Compared with the popular leaky integrate-and-fire (LIF) model, KLIF adds a learnable scaling factor to dynamically update the slope and width of the surrogate gradient curve during training and incorporates a ReLU activation function that selectively delivers membrane potential to spike firing and resetting. The proposed spiking unit is evaluated on both static MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10 datasets, as well as neuromorphic N-MNIST, CIFAR10-DVS, and DVS128-Gesture datasets. Experiments indicate that KLIF performs much better than LIF without introducing additional computational cost and achieves state-of-the-art performance on these datasets with few time steps. Also, KLIF is believed to be more biological plausible than LIF. The good performance of KLIF can make it completely replace the role of LIF in SNN for various tasks.
abstract_zh: 尖峰神经网络（SNN）因其处理时间信息的能力、低功耗和更高的生物学合理性而备受关注。然而，为 SNN 开发高效且高性能的学习算法仍然具有挑战性。人工神经网络 (ANN) 到 SNN 转换等方法可以将 ANN 转换为 SNN，性能损失较小，但需要长时间的模拟来近似速率编码。通过基于尖峰的反向传播（BP）（例如代理梯度近似）直接训练 SNN 更加灵活。然而现在，SNN 的性能与 ANN 相比并不具有竞争力。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于 k 的泄漏积分与激发（KLIF）神经元模型来提高 SNN 的学习能力。与流行的泄漏积分激发 (LIF) 模型相比，KLIF 增加了一个可学习的缩放因子，可在训练期间动态更新替代梯度曲线的斜率和宽度，并包含 ReLU 激活函数，可选择性地传递膜电位以进行尖峰激发和重置。所提出的尖峰单元在静态 MNIST、Fashion-MNIST、CIFAR-10 数据集以及神经形态 N-MNIST、CIFAR10-DVS 和 DVS128-Gesture 数据集上进行评估。实验表明，KLIF 的性能比 LIF 好得多，且无需引入额外的计算成本，并且只需很少的时间步即可在这些数据集上实现最先进的性能。此外，KLIF 被认为比 LIF 更具生物学合理性。 KLIF的良好性能可以使其完全替代SNN中LIF的作用来完成各种任务。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新型的基于可学习缩放因子的漏积分火（KLIF）神经元模型，以提升脉冲神经网络（SNN）的学习能力。
AI.motivation: 尽管脉冲神经网络（SNN）在处理时间信息和功耗方面具有优势，但其训练算法效率低且性能不如人工神经网络（ANN），现有方法如ANN到SNN转换需长时间模拟，而直接训练方法效果有限。
AI.method: KLIF在传统LIF模型基础上引入可学习缩放因子，动态调整替代梯度曲线的斜率和宽度，并结合ReLU激活函数选择性传递膜电位至脉冲发放与重置。
AI.result: KLIF在静态MNIST、Fashion-MNIST、CIFAR-10及神经形态数据集上均优于LIF，且计算成本更低，在少量时间步下达到SOTA性能。
AI.conclusion: KLIF通过优化梯度调节和膜电位传递机制，显著提升SNN性能，且生物合理性更高，可完全替代LIF用于多种任务。

Experiments indicate that KLIF performs much better than LIF without introducing additional computational cost and achieves state-of-the-art performance on these datasets with few time steps. Also, KLIF is believed to be more biological plausible than LIF. The good performance of KLIF can make it completely replace the role of LIF in SNN for various tasks. 尖峰神经网络（SNN）因其处理时间信息的能力、低功耗和更高的生物学合理性而备受关注。然而，为 SNN 开发高效且高性能的学习算法仍然具有挑战性。人工神经网络 (ANN) 到 SNN 转换等方法可以将 ANN 转换为 SNN，性能损失较小，但需要长时间的模拟来近似速率编码。通过基于尖峰的反向传播（BP）（例如代理梯度近似）直接训练 SNN 更加灵活。然而现在，SNN 的性能与 ANN 相比并不具有竞争力。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于 k 的泄漏积分与激发（KLIF）神经元模型来提高 SNN 的学习能力。与流行的泄漏积分激发 (LIF) 模型相比，KLIF 增加了一个可学习的缩放因子，可在训练期间动态更新替代梯度曲线的斜率和宽度，并包含 ReLU 激活函数，可选择性地传递膜电位以进行尖峰激发和重置。所提出的尖峰单元在静态 MNIST、Fashion-MNIST、CIFAR-10 数据集以及神经形态 N-MNIST、CIFAR10-DVS 和 DVS128-Gesture 数据集上进行评估。实验表明，KLIF 的性能比 LIF 好得多，且无需引入额外的计算成本，并且只需很少的时间步即可在这些数据集上实现最先进的性能。此外，KLIF 被认为比 LIF 更具生物学合理性。 KLIF的良好性能可以使其完全替代SNN中LIF的作用来完成各种任务。 本文提出一种新型的基于可学习缩放因子的漏积分火（KLIF）神经元模型，以提升脉冲神经网络（SNN）的学习能力。 Chunming Jiang,Yilei Zhang cs.NE,cs.CV

title: Characterizations of Network Auctions and Generalizations of VCG
title_zh: 网络拍卖的特点与VCG的概括
abstract: With the growth of networks, promoting products through social networks has become an important problem. For auctions in social networks, items are needed to be sold to agents in a network, where each agent can bid and also diffuse the sale information to her neighbors. Thus, the agents' social relations are intervened with their bids in the auctions. In network auctions, the classical VCG mechanism fails to retain key properties. In order to better understand network auctions, in this paper, we characterize network auctions for the single-unit setting with respect to weak budget balance, individual rationality, incentive compatibility, efficiency, and other properties. For example, we present sufficient conditions for mechanisms to be efficient and (weakly) incentive compatible. With the help of these properties and new concepts such as rewards, participation rewards, and so on, we show how to design efficient mechanisms to satisfy incentive compatibility as much as possible, and incentive compatibility mechanisms to maximize the revenue. Our results provide insights into understanding auctions in social networks.
summary: With the growth of networks, promoting products through social networks has become an important problem. For auctions in social networks, items are needed to be sold to agents in a network, where each agent can bid and also diffuse the sale information to her neighbors. Thus, the agents' social relations are intervened with their bids in the auctions. In network auctions, the classical VCG mechanism fails to retain key properties. In order to better understand network auctions, in this paper, we characterize network auctions for the single-unit setting with respect to weak budget balance, individual rationality, incentive compatibility, efficiency, and other properties. For example, we present sufficient conditions for mechanisms to be efficient and (weakly) incentive compatible. With the help of these properties and new concepts such as rewards, participation rewards, and so on, we show how to design efficient mechanisms to satisfy incentive compatibility as much as possible, and incentive compatibility mechanisms to maximize the revenue. Our results provide insights into understanding auctions in social networks.
abstract_zh: 随着网络的发展，通过社交网络推广产品已成为一个重要问题。对于社交网络中的拍卖，需要将物品出售给网络中的代理，每个代理都可以出价并将销售信息传播给邻居。因此，代理人的社会关系受到他们在拍卖中的出价的影响。在网络拍卖中，经典的 VCG 机制无法保留关键属性。为了更好地理解网络拍卖，在本文中，我们从弱预算平衡、个体理性、激励相容性、效率和其他属性方面描述了单一单位环境下的网络拍卖。例如，我们为机制的高效和（弱）激励相容提供了充分的条件。借助这些属性和奖励、参与奖励等新概念，我们展示了如何设计有效的机制来尽可能满足激励相容性，以及如何设计激励相容性机制来最大化收入。我们的结果为理解社交网络中的拍卖提供了见解。

AI.tldr: 本文研究社交网络拍卖的特性，提出传统VCG机制的不足，并设计满足激励相容与效率的拍卖机制。
AI.motivation: 社交网络中产品推广需考虑用户社交关系对拍卖的影响，传统拍卖机制无法适应网络环境下的激励相容与效率需求。
AI.method: 通过分析弱预算平衡、个人理性等属性，结合奖励机制设计，提出高效且近似激励相容的拍卖方案。
AI.result: 确定了机制效率与激励相容的充分条件，展示了如何设计兼顾效率与收益最大化的网络拍卖机制。
AI.conclusion: 研究为社交网络拍卖提供了理论框架，揭示了机制设计需平衡社交传播与经济激励的关键规律。

title: Scalable Prompt Generation for Semi-supervised Learning with Language Models
title_zh: 使用语言模型进行半监督学习的可扩展提示生成
abstract: Prompt-based learning methods in semi-supervised learning (SSL) settings have been shown to be effective on multiple natural language understanding (NLU) datasets and tasks in the literature. However, manually designing multiple prompts and verbalizers requires domain knowledge and human effort, making it difficult and expensive to scale across different datasets. In this paper, we propose two methods to automatically design multiple prompts and integrate automatic verbalizer in SSL settings without sacrificing performance. The first method uses various demonstration examples with learnable continuous prompt tokens to create diverse prompt models. The second method uses a varying number of soft prompt tokens to encourage language models to learn different prompts. For the verbalizer, we use the prototypical verbalizer to replace the manual one. In summary, we obtained the best average accuracy of 73.2% (a relative improvement of 2.52% over even the previous state-of-the-art SSL method with manual prompts and verbalizers) in different few-shot learning settings.
summary: Prompt-based learning methods in semi-supervised learning (SSL) settings have been shown to be effective on multiple natural language understanding (NLU) datasets and tasks in the literature. However, manually designing multiple prompts and verbalizers requires domain knowledge and human effort, making it difficult and expensive to scale across different datasets. In this paper, we propose two methods to automatically design multiple prompts and integrate automatic verbalizer in SSL settings without sacrificing performance. The first method uses various demonstration examples with learnable continuous prompt tokens to create diverse prompt models. The second method uses a varying number of soft prompt tokens to encourage language models to learn different prompts. For the verbalizer, we use the prototypical verbalizer to replace the manual one. In summary, we obtained the best average accuracy of 73.2% (a relative improvement of 2.52% over even the previous state-of-the-art SSL method with manual prompts and verbalizers) in different few-shot learning settings.
abstract_zh: 半监督学习 (SSL) 设置中基于提示的学习方法已被证明对文献中的多个自然语言理解 (NLU) 数据集和任务有效。然而，手动设计多个提示和语言器需要领域知识和人力，使得跨不同数据集扩展变得困难且昂贵。在本文中，我们提出了两种方法来自动设计多个提示并在 SSL 设置中集成自动语言器，而不牺牲性能。第一种方法使用具有可学习的连续提示标记的各种演示示例来创建不同的提示模型。第二种方法使用不同数量的软提示标记来鼓励语言模型学习不同的提示。对于语言器，我们使用原型语言器来代替手动语言器。总而言之，我们在不同的少样本学习设置中获得了 73.2% 的最佳平均准确率（甚至比之前具有手动提示和语言器的最先进 SSL 方法相对提高了 2.52%）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种自动设计提示和集成自动释义器的方法，以提升半监督学习中语言模型的性能。
AI.motivation: 手动设计多组提示和释义器需要领域知识和大量人力，导致跨数据集扩展困难且成本高昂。
AI.method: 第一种方法通过可学习的连续提示令牌和多样化示例生成多样化的提示模型；第二种方法使用不同数量的软提示令牌促使模型学习不同提示。同时采用原型释义器替代手动设计。
AI.result: 在不同少样本学习设置中，模型平均准确率达到73.2%，较之前最先进的手动提示方法提升2.52%。
AI.conclusion: 自动化的提示生成方法在保持性能的同时显著降低了人工成本，为半监督学习提供了可扩展解决方案。

authors_text: Feng Qian,Sifeng He,Honghao Huang,Huanyu Ma,Xiaobo Zhang,Lei Yang
title: Web Photo Source Identification based on Neural Enhanced Camera Fingerprint
title_zh: 基于神经增强相机指纹的网络照片来源识别
comment: Accepted by WWW2023 (https://www2023.thewebconf.org/). Codes are all publicly available at https://github.com/PhotoNecf/PhotoNecf
comments: Accepted by WWW2023 (https://www2023.thewebconf.org/). Codes are all publicly available at https://github.com/PhotoNecf/PhotoNecf
abstract: With the growing popularity of smartphone photography in recent years, web photos play an increasingly important role in all walks of life. Source camera identification of web photos aims to establish a reliable linkage from the captured images to their source cameras, and has a broad range of applications, such as image copyright protection, user authentication, investigated evidence verification, etc. This paper presents an innovative and practical source identification framework that employs neural-network enhanced sensor pattern noise to trace back web photos efficiently while ensuring security. Our proposed framework consists of three main stages: initial device fingerprint registration, fingerprint extraction and cryptographic connection establishment while taking photos, and connection verification between photos and source devices. By incorporating metric learning and frequency consistency into the deep network design, our proposed fingerprint extraction algorithm achieves state-of-the-art performance on modern smartphone photos for reliable source identification. Meanwhile, we also propose several optimization sub-modules to prevent fingerprint leakage and improve accuracy and efficiency. Finally for practical system design, two cryptographic schemes are introduced to reliably identify the correlation between registered fingerprint and verified photo fingerprint, i.e. fuzzy extractor and zero-knowledge proof (ZKP). The codes for fingerprint extraction network and benchmark dataset with modern smartphone cameras photos are all publicly available at https://github.com/PhotoNecf/PhotoNecf.
summary: With the growing popularity of smartphone photography in recent years, web photos play an increasingly important role in all walks of life. Source camera identification of web photos aims to establish a reliable linkage from the captured images to their source cameras, and has a broad range of applications, such as image copyright protection, user authentication, investigated evidence verification, etc. This paper presents an innovative and practical source identification framework that employs neural-network enhanced sensor pattern noise to trace back web photos efficiently while ensuring security. Our proposed framework consists of three main stages: initial device fingerprint registration, fingerprint extraction and cryptographic connection establishment while taking photos, and connection verification between photos and source devices. By incorporating metric learning and frequency consistency into the deep network design, our proposed fingerprint extraction algorithm achieves state-of-the-art performance on modern smartphone photos for reliable source identification. Meanwhile, we also propose several optimization sub-modules to prevent fingerprint leakage and improve accuracy and efficiency. Finally for practical system design, two cryptographic schemes are introduced to reliably identify the correlation between registered fingerprint and verified photo fingerprint, i.e. fuzzy extractor and zero-knowledge proof (ZKP). The codes for fingerprint extraction network and benchmark dataset with modern smartphone cameras photos are all publicly available at https://github.com/PhotoNecf/PhotoNecf.
abstract_zh: 近年来，随着智能手机摄影的日益普及，网络照片在各行各业中发挥着越来越重要的作用。网络照片的源相机识别旨在建立从捕获的图像到源相机的可靠链接，并且具有广泛的应用，例如图像版权保护、用户身份验证、调查证据验证等。本文提出了一种创新且实用的源识别框架，该框架采用神经网络增强的传感器模式噪声来有效地追溯网络照片，同时确保安全性。我们提出的框架由三个主要阶段组成：初始设备指纹注册、拍照时的指纹提取和加密连接建立以及照片和源设备之间的连接验证。通过将度量学习和频率一致性纳入深度网络设计中，我们提出的指纹提取算法在现代智能手机照片上实现了最先进的性能，以实现可靠的来源识别。同时，我们还提出了几个优化子模块来防止指纹泄漏并提高准确性和效率。最后，在实际系统设计中，引入了两种密码方案来可靠地识别注册指纹和已验证照片指纹之间的相关性，即模糊提取器和零知识证明（ZKP）。指纹提取网络的代码和现代智能手机相机照片的基准数据集均可在 https://github.com/PhotoNecf/PhotoNecf 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经网络增强传感器噪声的网络照片来源识别框架，通过设备指纹注册、提取及加密验证实现高效安全的来源追溯。
AI.motivation: 随着智能手机摄影普及，网络照片来源识别在版权保护、用户认证等场景需求迫切，但现有方法存在指纹泄露风险和识别准确率不足的问题。
AI.method: 框架分三阶段：设备指纹注册、基于度量学习和频率一致性的指纹提取算法、结合模糊提取器和零知识证明的加密验证，包含多项防泄露优化模块。
AI.result: 在现代智能手机照片数据集上达到SOTA性能，公开代码和数据集，验证方案兼顾准确率与安全性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架通过神经网络增强的传感器噪声分析和密码学方案，实现了高效可靠的网络照片来源识别，为实际应用提供安全解决方案。

search_text: Web Photo Source Identification based on Neural Enhanced Camera Fingerprint 基于神经增强相机指纹的网络照片来源识别 With the growing popularity of smartphone photography in recent years, web photos play an increasingly important role in all walks of life. Source camera identification of web photos aims to establish a reliable linkage from the captured images to their source cameras, and has a broad range of applications, such as image copyright protection, user authentication, investigated evidence verification, etc. This paper presents an innovative and practical source identification framework that employs neural-network enhanced sensor pattern noise to trace back web photos efficiently while ensuring security. Our proposed framework consists of three main stages: initial device fingerprint registration, fingerprint extraction and cryptographic connection establishment while taking photos, and connection verification between photos and source devices. By incorporating metric learning and frequency consistency into the deep network design, our proposed fingerprint extraction algorithm achieves state-of-the-art performance on modern smartphone photos for reliable source identification. Meanwhile, we also propose several optimization sub-modules to prevent fingerprint leakage and improve accuracy and efficiency. Finally for practical system design, two cryptographic schemes are introduced to reliably identify the correlation between registered fingerprint and verified photo fingerprint, i.e. fuzzy extractor and zero-knowledge proof (ZKP). The codes for fingerprint extraction network and benchmark dataset with modern smartphone cameras photos are all publicly available at https://github.com/PhotoNecf/PhotoNecf. 近年来，随着智能手机摄影的日益普及，网络照片在各行各业中发挥着越来越重要的作用。网络照片的源相机识别旨在建立从捕获的图像到源相机的可靠链接，并且具有广泛的应用，例如图像版权保护、用户身份验证、调查证据验证等。本文提出了一种创新且实用的源识别框架，该框架采用神经网络增强的传感器模式噪声来有效地追溯网络照片，同时确保安全性。我们提出的框架由三个主要阶段组成：初始设备指纹注册、拍照时的指纹提取和加密连接建立以及照片和源设备之间的连接验证。通过将度量学习和频率一致性纳入深度网络设计中，我们提出的指纹提取算法在现代智能手机照片上实现了最先进的性能，以实现可靠的来源识别。同时，我们还提出了几个优化子模块来防止指纹泄漏并提高准确性和效率。最后，在实际系统设计中，引入了两种密码方案来可靠地识别注册指纹和已验证照片指纹之间的相关性，即模糊提取器和零知识证明（ZKP）。指纹提取网络的代码和现代智能手机相机照片的基准数据集均可在 https://github.com/PhotoNecf/PhotoNecf 上公开获取。 本文提出一种基于神经网络增强传感器噪声的网络照片来源识别框架，通过设备指纹注册、提取及加密验证实现高效安全的来源追溯。 Feng Qian,Sifeng He,Honghao Huang,Huanyu Ma,Xiaobo Zhang,Lei Yang cs.CV

authors_text: Yash Kant,Aliaksandr Siarohin,Riza Alp Guler,Menglei Chai,Jian Ren,Sergey Tulyakov,Igor Gilitschenski
title: Invertible Neural Skinning
title_zh: 可逆神经蒙皮
abstract: Building animatable and editable models of clothed humans from raw 3D scans and poses is a challenging problem. Existing reposing methods suffer from the limited expressiveness of Linear Blend Skinning (LBS), require costly mesh extraction to generate each new pose, and typically do not preserve surface correspondences across different poses. In this work, we introduce Invertible Neural Skinning (INS) to address these shortcomings. To maintain correspondences, we propose a Pose-conditioned Invertible Network (PIN) architecture, which extends the LBS process by learning additional pose-varying deformations. Next, we combine PIN with a differentiable LBS module to build an expressive and end-to-end Invertible Neural Skinning (INS) pipeline. We demonstrate the strong performance of our method by outperforming the state-of-the-art reposing techniques on clothed humans and preserving surface correspondences, while being an order of magnitude faster. We also perform an ablation study, which shows the usefulness of our pose-conditioning formulation, and our qualitative results display that INS can rectify artefacts introduced by LBS well. See our webpage for more details: https://yashkant.github.io/invertible-neural-skinning/
summary: Building animatable and editable models of clothed humans from raw 3D scans and poses is a challenging problem. Existing reposing methods suffer from the limited expressiveness of Linear Blend Skinning (LBS), require costly mesh extraction to generate each new pose, and typically do not preserve surface correspondences across different poses. In this work, we introduce Invertible Neural Skinning (INS) to address these shortcomings. To maintain correspondences, we propose a Pose-conditioned Invertible Network (PIN) architecture, which extends the LBS process by learning additional pose-varying deformations. Next, we combine PIN with a differentiable LBS module to build an expressive and end-to-end Invertible Neural Skinning (INS) pipeline. We demonstrate the strong performance of our method by outperforming the state-of-the-art reposing techniques on clothed humans and preserving surface correspondences, while being an order of magnitude faster. We also perform an ablation study, which shows the usefulness of our pose-conditioning formulation, and our qualitative results display that INS can rectify artefacts introduced by LBS well. See our webpage for more details: https://yashkant.github.io/invertible-neural-skinning/
abstract_zh: 根据原始 3D 扫描和姿势构建可动画和可编辑的穿着人类模型是一个具有挑战性的问题。现有的重置方法受到线性混合蒙皮（LBS）表达能力有限的影响，需要昂贵的网格提取来生成每个新姿势，并且通常不保留不同姿势之间的表面对应关系。在这项工作中，我们引入了可逆神经蒙皮（INS）来解决这些缺点。为了保持对应关系，我们提出了一种姿势条件可逆网络（PIN）架构，它通过学习额外的姿势变化变形来扩展 LBS 过程。接下来，我们将 PIN 与可微分的 LBS 模块结合起来，构建一个富有表现力的端到端可逆神经蒙皮 (INS) 管道。我们证明了我们的方法的强大性能，超越了最先进的在穿着衣服的人身上的放置技术并保留了表面对应关系，同时速度快了一个数量级。我们还进行了一项消融研究，这表明了我们的姿势调节公式的有用性，并且我们的定性结果表明 INS 可以很好地纠正 LBS 引入的伪影。请参阅我们的网页了解更多详细信息：https://yashkant.github.io/invertible-neural-skinning/

AI.tldr: 本文提出可逆神经蒙皮（INS）方法，解决从3D扫描和姿势中构建可动画、可编辑的穿着人类模型的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有方法受限于线性混合蒙皮（LBS）表达能力不足、需高成本网格提取且无法保持跨姿势表面对应关系。
AI.method: 设计基于姿势的可逆网络（PIN）扩展LBS，结合可微LBS模块构建端到端INS流程，学习姿态相关变形并保持表面对应。
AI.result: 在保持表面对应关系的前提下，性能优于现有技术且速度快10倍，消融实验验证姿势条件有效性，并能修正LBS引入的伪影。
AI.conclusion: INS通过可逆神经网络有效解决LBS局限性，为服装人体模型的高效重新定位提供新方案。

authors_text: Jingzong Li,Yik Hong Cai,Libin Liu,Yu Mao,Chun Jason Xue,Hong Xu
title: Moby: Empowering 2D Models for Efficient Point Cloud Analytics on the Edge
title_zh: Moby：支持 2D 模型在边缘进行高效点云分析
comment: Accepted to ACM International Conference on Multimedia (MM) 2023
comments: Accepted to ACM International Conference on Multimedia (MM) 2023
abstract: 3D object detection plays a pivotal role in many applications, most notably autonomous driving and robotics. These applications are commonly deployed on edge devices to promptly interact with the environment, and often require near real-time response. With limited computation power, it is challenging to execute 3D detection on the edge using highly complex neural networks. Common approaches such as offloading to the cloud induce significant latency overheads due to the large amount of point cloud data during transmission. To resolve the tension between wimpy edge devices and compute-intensive inference workloads, we explore the possibility of empowering fast 2D detection to extrapolate 3D bounding boxes. To this end, we present Moby, a novel system that demonstrates the feasibility and potential of our approach. We design a transformation pipeline for Moby that generates 3D bounding boxes efficiently and accurately based on 2D detection results without running 3D detectors. Further, we devise a frame offloading scheduler that decides when to launch the 3D detector judiciously in the cloud to avoid the errors from accumulating. Extensive evaluations on NVIDIA Jetson TX2 with real-world autonomous driving datasets demonstrate that Moby offers up to 91.9% latency improvement with modest accuracy loss over state of the art.
summary: 3D object detection plays a pivotal role in many applications, most notably autonomous driving and robotics. These applications are commonly deployed on edge devices to promptly interact with the environment, and often require near real-time response. With limited computation power, it is challenging to execute 3D detection on the edge using highly complex neural networks. Common approaches such as offloading to the cloud induce significant latency overheads due to the large amount of point cloud data during transmission. To resolve the tension between wimpy edge devices and compute-intensive inference workloads, we explore the possibility of empowering fast 2D detection to extrapolate 3D bounding boxes. To this end, we present Moby, a novel system that demonstrates the feasibility and potential of our approach. We design a transformation pipeline for Moby that generates 3D bounding boxes efficiently and accurately based on 2D detection results without running 3D detectors. Further, we devise a frame offloading scheduler that decides when to launch the 3D detector judiciously in the cloud to avoid the errors from accumulating. Extensive evaluations on NVIDIA Jetson TX2 with real-world autonomous driving datasets demonstrate that Moby offers up to 91.9% latency improvement with modest accuracy loss over state of the art.
abstract_zh: 3D 物体检测在许多应用中发挥着关键作用，尤其是自动驾驶和机器人技术。这些应用程序通常部署在边缘设备上，以便与环境快速交互，并且通常需要近乎实时的响应。由于计算能力有限，使用高度复杂的神经网络在边缘执行 3D 检测具有挑战性。由于传输过程中存在大量点云数据，诸如卸载到云端之类的常见方法会导致显着的延迟开销。为了解决弱边缘设备和计算密集型推理工作负载之间的紧张关系，我们探索了支持快速 2D 检测以推断 3D 边界框的可能性。为此，我们提出了 Moby，一个新颖的系统，展示了我们方法的可行性和潜力。我们为 Moby 设计了一个转换管道，可以根据 2D 检测结果高效准确地生成 3D 边界框，而无需运行 3D 检测器。此外，我们设计了一个帧卸载调度程序，可以明智地决定何时在云端启动 3D 检测器，以避免错误累积。使用真实自动驾驶数据集对 NVIDIA Jetson TX2 进行的广泛评估表明，与现有技术相比，Moby 的延迟改善幅度高达 91.9%，但精度损失不大。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Moby系统，通过增强2D检测模型实现边缘设备上的高效点云分析，解决3D目标检测的计算瓶颈问题。
AI.motivation: 边缘设备计算能力有限，传统3D检测模型复杂度高导致延迟大，云端处理又因点云数据量大而引入显著延迟，需寻找轻量化解决方案。
AI.method: 设计Moby系统，包含基于2D检测结果生成3D边界框的转换流水线，以及智能调度云端3D检测的帧卸载策略，减少计算负载与误差累积。
AI.result: 在NVIDIA Jetson TX2上实测显示，Moby相比现有方法延迟降低91.9%，且精度损失较小。
AI.conclusion: Moby通过2D模型赋能有效平衡了边缘设备的计算限制与3D检测需求，为实时场景提供高效解决方案。

search_text: Moby: Empowering 2D Models for Efficient Point Cloud Analytics on the Edge Moby：支持 2D 模型在边缘进行高效点云分析 3D object detection plays a pivotal role in many applications, most notably autonomous driving and robotics. These applications are commonly deployed on edge devices to promptly interact with the environment, and often require near real-time response. With limited computation power, it is challenging to execute 3D detection on the edge using highly complex neural networks. Common approaches such as offloading to the cloud induce significant latency overheads due to the large amount of point cloud data during transmission. To resolve the tension between wimpy edge devices and compute-intensive inference workloads, we explore the possibility of empowering fast 2D detection to extrapolate 3D bounding boxes. To this end, we present Moby, a novel system that demonstrates the feasibility and potential of our approach. We design a transformation pipeline for Moby that generates 3D bounding boxes efficiently and accurately based on 2D detection results without running 3D detectors. Further, we devise a frame offloading scheduler that decides when to launch the 3D detector judiciously in the cloud to avoid the errors from accumulating. Extensive evaluations on NVIDIA Jetson TX2 with real-world autonomous driving datasets demonstrate that Moby offers up to 91.9% latency improvement with modest accuracy loss over state of the art. 3D 物体检测在许多应用中发挥着关键作用，尤其是自动驾驶和机器人技术。这些应用程序通常部署在边缘设备上，以便与环境快速交互，并且通常需要近乎实时的响应。由于计算能力有限，使用高度复杂的神经网络在边缘执行 3D 检测具有挑战性。由于传输过程中存在大量点云数据，诸如卸载到云端之类的常见方法会导致显着的延迟开销。为了解决弱边缘设备和计算密集型推理工作负载之间的紧张关系，我们探索了支持快速 2D 检测以推断 3D 边界框的可能性。为此，我们提出了 Moby，一个新颖的系统，展示了我们方法的可行性和潜力。我们为 Moby 设计了一个转换管道，可以根据 2D 检测结果高效准确地生成 3D 边界框，而无需运行 3D 检测器。此外，我们设计了一个帧卸载调度程序，可以明智地决定何时在云端启动 3D 检测器，以避免错误累积。使用真实自动驾驶数据集对 NVIDIA Jetson TX2 进行的广泛评估表明，与现有技术相比，Moby 的延迟改善幅度高达 91.9%，但精度损失不大。 本文提出Moby系统，通过增强2D检测模型实现边缘设备上的高效点云分析，解决3D目标检测的计算瓶颈问题。 Jingzong Li,Yik Hong Cai,Libin Liu,Yu Mao,Chun Jason Xue,Hong Xu cs.NI,cs.CV

title: Domain Agnostic Pipeline for Retina Vessel Segmentation
title_zh: 用于视网膜血管分割的领域无关管道
abstract: Automatic segmentation of retina vessels plays a pivotal role in clinical diagnosis of prevalent eye diseases, such as, Diabetic Retinopathy or Age-related Macular Degeneration. Due to the complex construction of blood vessels, with drastically varying thicknesses, accurate vessel segmentation can be quite a challenging task. In this work we show that it is possible to achieve near state-of-the-art performance, by crafting a careful thought pre-processing pipeline, without having to resort to complex networks and/or training routines. We also show that our model is able to maintain the same high segmentation performance across different datasets, very poor quality fundus images, as well as images of severe pathological cases. Code and models featured in this paper can be downloaded from http://github.com/farrell236/retina_segmentation. We also demonstrate the potential of our model at http://lazarus.ddns.net:8502.
summary: Automatic segmentation of retina vessels plays a pivotal role in clinical diagnosis of prevalent eye diseases, such as, Diabetic Retinopathy or Age-related Macular Degeneration. Due to the complex construction of blood vessels, with drastically varying thicknesses, accurate vessel segmentation can be quite a challenging task. In this work we show that it is possible to achieve near state-of-the-art performance, by crafting a careful thought pre-processing pipeline, without having to resort to complex networks and/or training routines. We also show that our model is able to maintain the same high segmentation performance across different datasets, very poor quality fundus images, as well as images of severe pathological cases. Code and models featured in this paper can be downloaded from http://github.com/farrell236/retina_segmentation. We also demonstrate the potential of our model at http://lazarus.ddns.net:8502.
abstract_zh: 视网膜血管的自动分割在糖尿病视网膜病变或年龄相关性黄斑变性等常见眼部疾病的临床诊断中发挥着关键作用。由于血管结构复杂，厚度变化很大，准确的血管分割可能是一项相当具有挑战性的任务。在这项工作中，我们表明，通过精心设计预处理管道，无需求助于复杂的网络和/或训练例程，就可以实现接近最先进的性能。我们还表明，我们的模型能够在不同的数据集、质量非常差的眼底图像以及严重病理病例的图像中保持相同的高分割性能。本文中的代码和模型可以从http://github.com/farrell236/retina_segmentation下载。我们还在 http://lazarus.ddns.net:8502 上展示了我们模型的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需复杂网络和训练流程的视网膜血管分割预处理管道，实现了跨数据集和病理图像的高性能分割。
AI.motivation: 视网膜血管结构复杂且厚度变化大，现有方法依赖复杂模型或训练流程，难以适应不同质量数据集和病理图像的分割需求。
AI.method: 通过精心设计的预处理管道，无需使用复杂网络架构或训练策略，直接提升分割性能并保证跨域适应性。
AI.result: 在多种数据集、低质量眼底图像及严重病理案例中均达到近状态-of-the-art分割效果，代码和模型已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了预处理管道在视网膜血管分割中的有效性，为跨域医学图像分析提供了轻量化解决方案。

title: Cost-effective Models for Detecting Depression from Speech
title_zh: 从言语中检测抑郁症的经济有效的模型
abstract: Depression is the most common psychological disorder and is considered as a leading cause of disability and suicide worldwide. An automated system capable of detecting signs of depression in human speech can contribute to ensuring timely and effective mental health care for individuals suffering from the disorder. Developing such automated system requires accurate machine learning models, capable of capturing signs of depression. However, state-of-the-art models based on deep acoustic representations require abundant data, meticulous selection of features, and rigorous training; the procedure involves enormous computational resources. In this work, we explore the effectiveness of two different acoustic feature groups - conventional hand-curated and deep representation features, for predicting the severity of depression from speech. We explore the relevance of possible contributing factors to the models' performance, including gender of the individual, severity of the disorder, content and length of speech. Our findings suggest that models trained on conventional acoustic features perform equally well or better than the ones trained on deep representation features at significantly lower computational cost, irrespective of other factors, e.g. content and length of speech, gender of the speaker and severity of the disorder. This makes such models a better fit for deployment where availability of computational resources is restricted, such as real time depression monitoring applications in smart devices.
summary: Depression is the most common psychological disorder and is considered as a leading cause of disability and suicide worldwide. An automated system capable of detecting signs of depression in human speech can contribute to ensuring timely and effective mental health care for individuals suffering from the disorder. Developing such automated system requires accurate machine learning models, capable of capturing signs of depression. However, state-of-the-art models based on deep acoustic representations require abundant data, meticulous selection of features, and rigorous training; the procedure involves enormous computational resources. In this work, we explore the effectiveness of two different acoustic feature groups - conventional hand-curated and deep representation features, for predicting the severity of depression from speech. We explore the relevance of possible contributing factors to the models' performance, including gender of the individual, severity of the disorder, content and length of speech. Our findings suggest that models trained on conventional acoustic features perform equally well or better than the ones trained on deep representation features at significantly lower computational cost, irrespective of other factors, e.g. content and length of speech, gender of the speaker and severity of the disorder. This makes such models a better fit for deployment where availability of computational resources is restricted, such as real time depression monitoring applications in smart devices.
abstract_zh: 抑郁症是最常见的心理疾病，被认为是全世界残疾和自杀的主要原因。能够检测人类言语中抑郁症迹象的自动化系统有助于确保为患有抑郁症的个人提供及时有效的心理保健。开发这样的自动化系统需要准确的机器学习模型，能够捕捉抑郁症的迹象。然而，基于深度声学表征的最先进模型需要丰富的数据、精心的特征选择和严格的训练；该过程涉及大量的计算资源。在这项工作中，我们探索了两种不同的声学特征组（传统的手工策划特征和深度表征特征）在通过语音预测抑郁症严重程度方面的有效性。我们探讨了模型表现的可能影响因素的相关性，包括个体的性别、疾病的严重程度、演讲的内容和长度。我们的研究结果表明，无论其他因素如何，在传统声学特征上训练的模型与在深度表示特征上训练的模型表现相同或更好，且计算成本显着降低。演讲的内容和长度、演讲者的性别以及疾病的严重程度。这使得此类模型更适合在计算资源的可用性受到限制的情况下部署，例如智能设备中的实时抑郁症监测应用程序。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了从语音中检测抑郁症的低成本模型，比较了传统声学特征与深度表示特征的效果。
AI.motivation: 现有的深度学习模型需要大量数据和计算资源，难以在资源受限的场景中部署，因此需寻找更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过对比传统手工特征与深度特征在不同因素（性别、病情严重程度、语音内容和长度）下的表现，评估模型性能。
AI.result: 传统声学特征模型在计算成本显著降低的情况下，性能与深度模型相当或更优，且不受其他因素影响。
AI.conclusion: 传统特征模型更适合部署于计算资源有限的场景，如智能设备实时监测应用。

tldr: 本文研究了从语音中检测抑郁症的低成本模型，比较了传统声学特征与深度表示特征的效果。
motivation: 现有的深度学习模型需要大量数据和计算资源，难以在资源受限的场景中部署，因此需寻找更高效的解决方案。
method: 通过对比传统手工特征与深度特征在不同因素（性别、病情严重程度、语音内容和长度）下的表现，评估模型性能。
result: 传统声学特征模型在计算成本显著降低的情况下，性能与深度模型相当或更优，且不受其他因素影响。
conclusion: 传统特征模型更适合部署于计算资源有限的场景，如智能设备实时监测应用。
search_text: Cost-effective Models for Detecting Depression from Speech 从言语中检测抑郁症的经济有效的模型 Depression is the most common psychological disorder and is considered as a leading cause of disability and suicide worldwide. An automated system capable of detecting signs of depression in human speech can contribute to ensuring timely and effective mental health care for individuals suffering from the disorder. Developing such automated system requires accurate machine learning models, capable of capturing signs of depression. However, state-of-the-art models based on deep acoustic representations require abundant data, meticulous selection of features, and rigorous training; the procedure involves enormous computational resources. In this work, we explore the effectiveness of two different acoustic feature groups - conventional hand-curated and deep representation features, for predicting the severity of depression from speech. We explore the relevance of possible contributing factors to the models' performance, including gender of the individual, severity of the disorder, content and length of speech. Our findings suggest that models trained on conventional acoustic features perform equally well or better than the ones trained on deep representation features at significantly lower computational cost, irrespective of other factors, e.g. content and length of speech, gender of the speaker and severity of the disorder. This makes such models a better fit for deployment where availability of computational resources is restricted, such as real time depression monitoring applications in smart devices. 抑郁症是最常见的心理疾病，被认为是全世界残疾和自杀的主要原因。能够检测人类言语中抑郁症迹象的自动化系统有助于确保为患有抑郁症的个人提供及时有效的心理保健。开发这样的自动化系统需要准确的机器学习模型，能够捕捉抑郁症的迹象。然而，基于深度声学表征的最先进模型需要丰富的数据、精心的特征选择和严格的训练；该过程涉及大量的计算资源。在这项工作中，我们探索了两种不同的声学特征组（传统的手工策划特征和深度表征特征）在通过语音预测抑郁症严重程度方面的有效性。我们探讨了模型表现的可能影响因素的相关性，包括个体的性别、疾病的严重程度、演讲的内容和长度。我们的研究结果表明，无论其他因素如何，在传统声学特征上训练的模型与在深度表示特征上训练的模型表现相同或更好，且计算成本显着降低。演讲的内容和长度、演讲者的性别以及疾病的严重程度。这使得此类模型更适合在计算资源的可用性受到限制的情况下部署，例如智能设备中的实时抑郁症监测应用程序。 本文研究了从语音中检测抑郁症的低成本模型，比较了传统声学特征与深度表示特征的效果。 Mashrura Tasnim,Jekaterina Novikova cs.SD,eess.AS,stat.ML

title: Bridge Damage Cause Estimation Using Multiple Images Based on Visual Question Answering
title_zh: 基于视觉问答的多图像桥梁损坏原因估计
abstract: In this paper, a bridge member damage cause estimation framework is proposed by calculating the image position using Structure from Motion (SfM) and acquiring its information via Visual Question Answering (VQA). For this, a VQA model was developed that uses bridge images for dataset creation and outputs the damage or member name and its existence based on the images and questions. In the developed model, the correct answer rate for questions requiring the member's name and the damage's name were 67.4% and 68.9%, respectively. The correct answer rate for questions requiring a yes/no answer was 99.1%. Based on the developed model, a damage cause estimation method was proposed. In the proposed method, the damage causes are narrowed down by inputting new questions to the VQA model, which are determined based on the surrounding images obtained via SfM and the results of the VQA model. Subsequently, the proposed method was then applied to an actual bridge and shown to be capable of determining damage and estimating its cause. The proposed method could be used to prevent damage causes from being overlooked, and practitioners could determine inspection focus areas, which could contribute to the improvement of maintenance techniques. In the future, it is expected to contribute to infrastructure diagnosis automation.
summary: In this paper, a bridge member damage cause estimation framework is proposed by calculating the image position using Structure from Motion (SfM) and acquiring its information via Visual Question Answering (VQA). For this, a VQA model was developed that uses bridge images for dataset creation and outputs the damage or member name and its existence based on the images and questions. In the developed model, the correct answer rate for questions requiring the member's name and the damage's name were 67.4% and 68.9%, respectively. The correct answer rate for questions requiring a yes/no answer was 99.1%. Based on the developed model, a damage cause estimation method was proposed. In the proposed method, the damage causes are narrowed down by inputting new questions to the VQA model, which are determined based on the surrounding images obtained via SfM and the results of the VQA model. Subsequently, the proposed method was then applied to an actual bridge and shown to be capable of determining damage and estimating its cause. The proposed method could be used to prevent damage causes from being overlooked, and practitioners could determine inspection focus areas, which could contribute to the improvement of maintenance techniques. In the future, it is expected to contribute to infrastructure diagnosis automation.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种桥梁构件损坏原因估计框架，通过使用运动结构（SfM）计算图像位置并通过视觉问答（VQA）获取其信息。为此，开发了一个 VQA 模型，该模型使用桥梁图像来创建数据集，并根据图像和问题输出损坏或成员名称及其存在性。在开发的模型中，需要成员姓名和损害姓名的问题的正确回答率分别为 67.4% 和 68.9%。需要回答是/否的问题的正确回答率为 99.1%。基于所开发的模型，提出了一种损伤原因估计方法。在所提出的方法中，通过向 VQA 模型输入新问题来缩小损坏原因的范围，这些新问题是根据 SfM 获得的周围图像和 VQA 模型的结果确定的。随后，所提出的方法被应用于实际桥梁，并被证明能够确定损坏并估计其原因。所提出的方法可用于防止忽视损坏原因，并且从业人员可以确定检查重点区域，这有助于维护技术的改进。未来有望为基础设施诊断自动化做出贡献。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多图像和视觉问答（VQA）的桥梁构件损伤原因估计框架。
AI.motivation: 现有桥梁检测方法易遗漏损伤原因，需提升检测准确性与效率以优化维护技术。
AI.method: 结合结构从运动（SfM）定位图像位置，并开发专用VQA模型，通过输入问题从图像中提取损伤信息，逐步缩小损伤原因范围。
AI.result: VQA模型对构件名称、损伤名称识别准确率分别为67.4%、68.9%，是/否问题准确率达99.1%；实际桥梁测试验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可辅助定位损伤原因，提升检测效率，未来有望推动基础设施诊断自动化。

authors_text: Elie Bursztein,Marina Zhang,Owen Vallis,Xinyu Jia,Alexey Kurakin
title: RETVec: Resilient and Efficient Text Vectorizer
title_zh: RETVec：弹性且高效的文本矢量化器
comment: 37th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2023)
comments: 37th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2023)
abstract: This paper describes RETVec, an efficient, resilient, and multilingual text vectorizer designed for neural-based text processing. RETVec combines a novel character encoding with an optional small embedding model to embed words into a 256-dimensional vector space. The RETVec embedding model is pre-trained using pair-wise metric learning to be robust against typos and character-level adversarial attacks. In this paper, we evaluate and compare RETVec to state-of-the-art vectorizers and word embeddings on popular model architectures and datasets. These comparisons demonstrate that RETVec leads to competitive, multilingual models that are significantly more resilient to typos and adversarial text attacks. RETVec is available under the Apache 2 license at https://github.com/google-research/retvec.
summary: This paper describes RETVec, an efficient, resilient, and multilingual text vectorizer designed for neural-based text processing. RETVec combines a novel character encoding with an optional small embedding model to embed words into a 256-dimensional vector space. The RETVec embedding model is pre-trained using pair-wise metric learning to be robust against typos and character-level adversarial attacks. In this paper, we evaluate and compare RETVec to state-of-the-art vectorizers and word embeddings on popular model architectures and datasets. These comparisons demonstrate that RETVec leads to competitive, multilingual models that are significantly more resilient to typos and adversarial text attacks. RETVec is available under the Apache 2 license at https://github.com/google-research/retvec.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 RETVec，这是一种高效、有弹性的多语言文本矢量化器，专为基于神经的文本处理而设计。 RETVec 将新颖的字符编码与可选的小型嵌入模型相结合，将单词嵌入到 256 维向量空间中。 RETVec 嵌入模型使用成对度量学习进行预训练，以抵御拼写错误和字符级对抗攻击。在本文中，我们在流行的模型架构和数据集上评估和比较 RETVec 与最先进的向量化器和词嵌入。这些比较表明，RETVec 带来了具有竞争力的多语言模型，这些模型对拼写错误和对抗性文本攻击的恢复能力显着增强。 RETVec 可在 Apache 2 许可证下使用，网址为 https://github.com/google-research/retvec。

AI.tldr: 本文提出RETVec，一种高效、鲁棒且支持多语言的文本向量化工具，通过结合字符编码和小型嵌入模型实现对文本的256维向量表示。
AI.motivation: 现有文本向量化方法在面对拼写错误和字符级对抗攻击时表现脆弱，且缺乏高效的多语言支持。
AI.method: 设计新型字符编码方案，结合预训练的小型嵌入模型，并通过成对度量学习增强模型对噪声和对抗样本的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在主流模型架构和数据集上，RETVec在保持竞争力的同时，显著优于现有方法，尤其在抗拼写错误和对抗攻击方面表现突出。
AI.conclusion: RETVec为多语言文本处理提供了高效且鲁棒的解决方案，代码已开源供学术研究使用。

authors_text: Ian Osband,Zheng Wen,Seyed Mohammad Asghari,Vikranth Dwaracherla,Morteza Ibrahimi,Xiuyuan Lu,Benjamin Van Roy
title: Approximate Thompson Sampling via Epistemic Neural Networks
title_zh: 通过认知神经网络进行近似汤普森采样
abstract: Thompson sampling (TS) is a popular heuristic for action selection, but it requires sampling from a posterior distribution. Unfortunately, this can become computationally intractable in complex environments, such as those modeled using neural networks. Approximate posterior samples can produce effective actions, but only if they reasonably approximate joint predictive distributions of outputs across inputs. Notably, accuracy of marginal predictive distributions does not suffice. Epistemic neural networks (ENNs) are designed to produce accurate joint predictive distributions. We compare a range of ENNs through computational experiments that assess their performance in approximating TS across bandit and reinforcement learning environments. The results indicate that ENNs serve this purpose well and illustrate how the quality of joint predictive distributions drives performance. Further, we demonstrate that the \textit{epinet} -- a small additive network that estimates uncertainty -- matches the performance of large ensembles at orders of magnitude lower computational cost. This enables effective application of TS with computation that scales gracefully to complex environments.
summary: Thompson sampling (TS) is a popular heuristic for action selection, but it requires sampling from a posterior distribution. Unfortunately, this can become computationally intractable in complex environments, such as those modeled using neural networks. Approximate posterior samples can produce effective actions, but only if they reasonably approximate joint predictive distributions of outputs across inputs. Notably, accuracy of marginal predictive distributions does not suffice. Epistemic neural networks (ENNs) are designed to produce accurate joint predictive distributions. We compare a range of ENNs through computational experiments that assess their performance in approximating TS across bandit and reinforcement learning environments. The results indicate that ENNs serve this purpose well and illustrate how the quality of joint predictive distributions drives performance. Further, we demonstrate that the \textit{epinet} -- a small additive network that estimates uncertainty -- matches the performance of large ensembles at orders of magnitude lower computational cost. This enables effective application of TS with computation that scales gracefully to complex environments.
abstract_zh: 汤普森采样（TS）是一种流行的动作选择启发式方法，但它需要从后验分布中采样。不幸的是，在复杂的环境中，例如使用神经网络建模的环境中，这可能会变得难以计算。近似后验样本可以产生有效的动作，但前提是它们合理地近似输入之间输出的联合预测分布。值得注意的是，边际预测分布的准确性还不够。认知神经网络（ENN）旨在产生准确的联合预测分布。我们通过计算实验比较了一系列 ENN，评估它们在强盗和强化学习环境中近似 TS 的性能。结果表明，ENN 很好地满足了这一目的，并说明了联合预测分布的质量如何驱动性能。此外，我们证明了 \textit{epinet} ——一个估计不确定性的小型加性网络 ——以低几个数量级的计算成本与大型集成的性能相匹配。这使得 TS 的有效应用和计算能够优雅地扩展到复杂的环境中。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过认知神经网络（ENN）近似汤普森采样（TS），以解决复杂环境中后验采样计算困难的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统汤普森采样在复杂环境（如神经网络建模场景）中因后验采样计算开销过大而难以应用，且现有近似方法对联合预测分布的准确性不足。
AI.method: 比较多种认知神经网络在bandit和强化学习任务中近似TS的性能，并提出计算成本更低的epinet结构（小型加法网络）替代大型集成模型。
AI.result: 实验表明ENN能有效近似TS，且epinet在保持性能的同时计算成本降低数个数量级。
AI.conclusion: 认知神经网络可有效支持复杂环境下的汤普森采样，其中epinet结构显著提升了计算效率。

search_text: Approximate Thompson Sampling via Epistemic Neural Networks 通过认知神经网络进行近似汤普森采样 Thompson sampling (TS) is a popular heuristic for action selection, but it requires sampling from a posterior distribution. Unfortunately, this can become computationally intractable in complex environments, such as those modeled using neural networks. Approximate posterior samples can produce effective actions, but only if they reasonably approximate joint predictive distributions of outputs across inputs. Notably, accuracy of marginal predictive distributions does not suffice. Epistemic neural networks (ENNs) are designed to produce accurate joint predictive distributions. We compare a range of ENNs through computational experiments that assess their performance in approximating TS across bandit and reinforcement learning environments. The results indicate that ENNs serve this purpose well and illustrate how the quality of joint predictive distributions drives performance. Further, we demonstrate that the \textit{epinet} -- a small additive network that estimates uncertainty -- matches the performance of large ensembles at orders of magnitude lower computational cost. This enables effective application of TS with computation that scales gracefully to complex environments. 汤普森采样（TS）是一种流行的动作选择启发式方法，但它需要从后验分布中采样。不幸的是，在复杂的环境中，例如使用神经网络建模的环境中，这可能会变得难以计算。近似后验样本可以产生有效的动作，但前提是它们合理地近似输入之间输出的联合预测分布。值得注意的是，边际预测分布的准确性还不够。认知神经网络（ENN）旨在产生准确的联合预测分布。我们通过计算实验比较了一系列 ENN，评估它们在强盗和强化学习环境中近似 TS 的性能。结果表明，ENN 很好地满足了这一目的，并说明了联合预测分布的质量如何驱动性能。此外，我们证明了 \textit{epinet} ——一个估计不确定性的小型加性网络 ——以低几个数量级的计算成本与大型集成的性能相匹配。这使得 TS 的有效应用和计算能够优雅地扩展到复杂的环境中。 本文提出通过认知神经网络（ENN）近似汤普森采样（TS），以解决复杂环境中后验采样计算困难的问题。 Ian Osband,Zheng Wen,Seyed Mohammad Asghari,Vikranth Dwaracherla,Morteza Ibrahimi,Xiuyuan Lu,Benjamin Van Roy cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Md Mahfuzur Rahman Siddiquee,Jay Shah,Teresa Wu,Catherine Chong,Todd J. Schwedt,Gina Dumkrieger,Simona Nikolova,Baoxin Li
title: Brainomaly: Unsupervised Neurologic Disease Detection Utilizing Unannotated T1-weighted Brain MR Images
title_zh: 脑瘤：利用未注释的 T1 加权脑 MR 图像进行无人监督的神经系统疾病检测
abstract: Harnessing the power of deep neural networks in the medical imaging domain is challenging due to the difficulties in acquiring large annotated datasets, especially for rare diseases, which involve high costs, time, and effort for annotation. Unsupervised disease detection methods, such as anomaly detection, can significantly reduce human effort in these scenarios. While anomaly detection typically focuses on learning from images of healthy subjects only, real-world situations often present unannotated datasets with a mixture of healthy and diseased subjects. Recent studies have demonstrated that utilizing such unannotated images can improve unsupervised disease and anomaly detection. However, these methods do not utilize knowledge specific to registered neuroimages, resulting in a subpar performance in neurologic disease detection. To address this limitation, we propose Brainomaly, a GAN-based image-to-image translation method specifically designed for neurologic disease detection. Brainomaly not only offers tailored image-to-image translation suitable for neuroimages but also leverages unannotated mixed images to achieve superior neurologic disease detection. Additionally, we address the issue of model selection for inference without annotated samples by proposing a pseudo-AUC metric, further enhancing Brainomaly's detection performance. Extensive experiments and ablation studies demonstrate that Brainomaly outperforms existing state-of-the-art unsupervised disease and anomaly detection methods by significant margins in Alzheimer's disease detection using a publicly available dataset and headache detection using an institutional dataset. The code is available from https://github.com/mahfuzmohammad/Brainomaly.
summary: Harnessing the power of deep neural networks in the medical imaging domain is challenging due to the difficulties in acquiring large annotated datasets, especially for rare diseases, which involve high costs, time, and effort for annotation. Unsupervised disease detection methods, such as anomaly detection, can significantly reduce human effort in these scenarios. While anomaly detection typically focuses on learning from images of healthy subjects only, real-world situations often present unannotated datasets with a mixture of healthy and diseased subjects. Recent studies have demonstrated that utilizing such unannotated images can improve unsupervised disease and anomaly detection. However, these methods do not utilize knowledge specific to registered neuroimages, resulting in a subpar performance in neurologic disease detection. To address this limitation, we propose Brainomaly, a GAN-based image-to-image translation method specifically designed for neurologic disease detection. Brainomaly not only offers tailored image-to-image translation suitable for neuroimages but also leverages unannotated mixed images to achieve superior neurologic disease detection. Additionally, we address the issue of model selection for inference without annotated samples by proposing a pseudo-AUC metric, further enhancing Brainomaly's detection performance. Extensive experiments and ablation studies demonstrate that Brainomaly outperforms existing state-of-the-art unsupervised disease and anomaly detection methods by significant margins in Alzheimer's disease detection using a publicly available dataset and headache detection using an institutional dataset. The code is available from https://github.com/mahfuzmohammad/Brainomaly.
abstract_zh: 由于获取大型注释数据集非常困难，在医学成像领域利用深度神经网络的力量具有挑战性，特别是对于罕见疾病，这涉及高昂的注释成本、时间和精力。无监督的疾病检测方法（例如异常检测）可以显着减少这些场景中的人力。虽然异常检测通常只专注于从健康受试者的图像中学习，但现实世界的情况通常会呈现包含健康和患病受试者混合的未注释数据集。最近的研究表明，利用此类未注释的图像可以改善无人监督的疾病和异常检测。然而，这些方法没有利用特定于注册神经图像的知识，导致神经系统疾病检测的性能不佳。为了解决这个限制，我们提出了 Brainomaly，一种基于 GAN 的图像到图像翻译方法，专门为神经系统疾病检测而设计。 Brainomaly 不仅提供适合神经图像的定制图像到图像转换，而且还利用未注释的混合图像来实现卓越的神经系统疾病检测。此外，我们通过提出伪 AUC 指标，解决了无需注释样本的推理模型选择问题，进一步增强了 Brainomaly 的检测性能。广泛的实验和消融研究表明，Brainomaly 在使用公开数据集的阿尔茨海默病检测和使用机构数据集的头痛检测方面明显优于现有最先进的无监督疾病和异常检测方法。该代码可从 https://github.com/mahfuzmohammad/Brainomaly 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的无监督方法Brainomaly，用于利用未标注的T1加权脑部MRI图像进行神经疾病检测。
AI.motivation: 医学影像领域中，罕见疾病的标注数据获取成本高且耗时，传统无监督异常检测方法未充分利用神经影像的注册知识，导致神经疾病检测效果不佳。
AI.method: 设计了一种针对神经影像的图像到图像转换方法Brainomaly，并引入伪AUC指标解决无标注样本下的模型选择问题，通过混合未标注的健康与患病图像提升检测性能。
AI.result: 在公开数据集（阿尔茨海默病检测）和机构数据集（偏头痛检测）上，Brainomaly显著优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: Brainomaly通过结合神经影像特性与无监督学习，在无需标注数据的情况下实现了更优的神经疾病检测效果。

authors_text: Chengzhe Sun,Shan Jia,Shuwei Hou,Ehab AlBadawy,Siwei Lyu
title: Exposing AI-Synthesized Human Voices Using Neural Vocoder Artifacts
title_zh: 使用神经声码器伪像揭示人工智能合成的人类声音
comment: Dataset and codes will be available at https://github.com/csun22/LibriVoc-Dataset
comments: Dataset and codes will be available at https://github.com/csun22/LibriVoc-Dataset
abstract: The advancements of AI-synthesized human voices have introduced a growing threat of impersonation and disinformation. It is therefore of practical importance to developdetection methods for synthetic human voices. This work proposes a new approach to detect synthetic human voices based on identifying artifacts of neural vocoders in audio signals. A neural vocoder is a specially designed neural network that synthesizes waveforms from temporal-frequency representations, e.g., mel-spectrograms. The neural vocoder is a core component in most DeepFake audio synthesis models. Hence the identification of neural vocoder processing implies that an audio sample may have been synthesized. To take advantage of the vocoder artifacts for synthetic human voice detection, we introduce a multi-task learning framework for a binary-class RawNet2 model that shares the front-end feature extractor with a vocoder identification module. We treat the vocoder identification as a pretext task to constrain the front-end feature extractor to focus on vocoder artifacts and provide discriminative features for the final binary classifier. Our experiments show that the improved RawNet2 model based on vocoder identification achieves an overall high classification performance on the binary task.
summary: The advancements of AI-synthesized human voices have introduced a growing threat of impersonation and disinformation. It is therefore of practical importance to developdetection methods for synthetic human voices. This work proposes a new approach to detect synthetic human voices based on identifying artifacts of neural vocoders in audio signals. A neural vocoder is a specially designed neural network that synthesizes waveforms from temporal-frequency representations, e.g., mel-spectrograms. The neural vocoder is a core component in most DeepFake audio synthesis models. Hence the identification of neural vocoder processing implies that an audio sample may have been synthesized. To take advantage of the vocoder artifacts for synthetic human voice detection, we introduce a multi-task learning framework for a binary-class RawNet2 model that shares the front-end feature extractor with a vocoder identification module. We treat the vocoder identification as a pretext task to constrain the front-end feature extractor to focus on vocoder artifacts and provide discriminative features for the final binary classifier. Our experiments show that the improved RawNet2 model based on vocoder identification achieves an overall high classification performance on the binary task.
abstract_zh: 人工智能合成人类声音的进步带来了日益严重的冒充和虚假信息威胁。因此，开发合成人声的检测方法具有重要的现实意义。这项工作提出了一种基于识别音频信号中神经声码器伪影来检测合成人类声音的新方法。神经声码器是一种专门设计的神经网络，它从时频表示（例如梅尔频谱图）合成波形。神经声码器是大多数 DeepFake 音频合成模型的核心组件。因此，神经声码器处理的识别意味着音频样本可能已经被合成。为了利用声码器工件进行合成人声检测，我们引入了用于二进制类 RawNet2 模型的多任务学习框架，该模型与声码器识别模块共享前端特征提取器。我们将声码器识别视为借口任务，以限制前端特征提取器专注于声码器伪影，并为最终的二元分类器提供判别性特征。我们的实验表明，基于声码器识别的改进的 RawNet2 模型在二元任务上实现了总体较高的分类性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经声码器伪影检测AI合成语音的方法。
AI.motivation: AI合成语音技术的发展带来了冒充和虚假信息的威胁，亟需开发有效的检测手段。
AI.method: 设计多任务学习框架，通过RawNet2模型共享前端特征提取器与声码器识别模块，利用声码器伪影进行二分类检测。
AI.result: 改进后的RawNet2模型在二分类任务中表现出色，验证了声码器伪影检测的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过声码器伪影识别可有效检测合成语音，为深度伪造音频检测提供新思路。

search_text: Exposing AI-Synthesized Human Voices Using Neural Vocoder Artifacts 使用神经声码器伪像揭示人工智能合成的人类声音 The advancements of AI-synthesized human voices have introduced a growing threat of impersonation and disinformation. It is therefore of practical importance to developdetection methods for synthetic human voices. This work proposes a new approach to detect synthetic human voices based on identifying artifacts of neural vocoders in audio signals. A neural vocoder is a specially designed neural network that synthesizes waveforms from temporal-frequency representations, e.g., mel-spectrograms. The neural vocoder is a core component in most DeepFake audio synthesis models. Hence the identification of neural vocoder processing implies that an audio sample may have been synthesized. To take advantage of the vocoder artifacts for synthetic human voice detection, we introduce a multi-task learning framework for a binary-class RawNet2 model that shares the front-end feature extractor with a vocoder identification module. We treat the vocoder identification as a pretext task to constrain the front-end feature extractor to focus on vocoder artifacts and provide discriminative features for the final binary classifier. Our experiments show that the improved RawNet2 model based on vocoder identification achieves an overall high classification performance on the binary task. 人工智能合成人类声音的进步带来了日益严重的冒充和虚假信息威胁。因此，开发合成人声的检测方法具有重要的现实意义。这项工作提出了一种基于识别音频信号中神经声码器伪影来检测合成人类声音的新方法。神经声码器是一种专门设计的神经网络，它从时频表示（例如梅尔频谱图）合成波形。神经声码器是大多数 DeepFake 音频合成模型的核心组件。因此，神经声码器处理的识别意味着音频样本可能已经被合成。为了利用声码器工件进行合成人声检测，我们引入了用于二进制类 RawNet2 模型的多任务学习框架，该模型与声码器识别模块共享前端特征提取器。我们将声码器识别视为借口任务，以限制前端特征提取器专注于声码器伪影，并为最终的二元分类器提供判别性特征。我们的实验表明，基于声码器识别的改进的 RawNet2 模型在二元任务上实现了总体较高的分类性能。 本文提出一种基于神经声码器伪影检测AI合成语音的方法。 Chengzhe Sun,Shan Jia,Shuwei Hou,Ehab AlBadawy,Siwei Lyu cs.SD,cs.MM,eess.AS

title: Data-Efficient Contrastive Self-supervised Learning: Most Beneficial Examples for Supervised Learning Contribute the Least
title_zh: 数据高效的对比自监督学习：监督学习最有益的例子贡献最少
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) learns high-quality representations from large pools of unlabeled training data. As datasets grow larger, it becomes crucial to identify the examples that contribute the most to learning such representations. This enables efficient SSL by reducing the volume of data required. Nevertheless, quantifying the value of examples for SSL has remained an open question. In this work, we address this problem for the first time, by proving that examples that contribute the most to contrastive SSL are those that have the most similar augmentations to other examples, in expectation. We provide rigorous guarantees for the generalization performance of contrastive learning on such subsets. Through extensive experiments, we show that we can safely exclude 20% of examples from CIFAR100 and 40% from STL10 and TinyImageNet, without affecting downstream task performance. In general, subsets selected by our method outperform random subsets by over 3% across these datasets. Interestingly, we also discover the subsets that contribute the most to contrastive learning are those that contribute the least to supervised learning. Code available at https://github.com/bigml-cs-ucla/sas-data-efficient-contrastive-learning.
summary: Self-supervised learning (SSL) learns high-quality representations from large pools of unlabeled training data. As datasets grow larger, it becomes crucial to identify the examples that contribute the most to learning such representations. This enables efficient SSL by reducing the volume of data required. Nevertheless, quantifying the value of examples for SSL has remained an open question. In this work, we address this problem for the first time, by proving that examples that contribute the most to contrastive SSL are those that have the most similar augmentations to other examples, in expectation. We provide rigorous guarantees for the generalization performance of contrastive learning on such subsets. Through extensive experiments, we show that we can safely exclude 20% of examples from CIFAR100 and 40% from STL10 and TinyImageNet, without affecting downstream task performance. In general, subsets selected by our method outperform random subsets by over 3% across these datasets. Interestingly, we also discover the subsets that contribute the most to contrastive learning are those that contribute the least to supervised learning. Code available at https://github.com/bigml-cs-ucla/sas-data-efficient-contrastive-learning.
abstract_zh: 自监督学习 (SSL) 从大量未标记的训练数据中学习高质量的表示。随着数据集变得越来越大，识别对学习此类表示贡献最大的示例变得至关重要。这可以通过减少所需的数据量来实现高效的 SSL。尽管如此，量化 SSL 示例的价值仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。在这项工作中，我们首次解决了这个问题，通过证明对对比 SSL 贡献最大的示例是那些与预期的其他示例具有最相似增强的示例。我们为此类子集上的对比学习的泛化性能提供严格的保证。通过大量的实验，我们表明我们可以安全地从 CIFAR100 中排除 20% 的示例，从 STL10 和 TinyImageNet 中排除 40% 的示例，而不会影响下游任务性能。一般来说，在这些数据集中，我们的方法选择的子集比随机子集的性能高出 3% 以上。有趣的是，我们还发现对对比学习贡献最大的子集是对监督学习贡献最小的子集。代码位于 https://github.com/bigml-cs-ucla/sas-data-efficient-contrastive-learning。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过识别对对比自监督学习（SSL）贡献最大的数据示例，实现更高效的数据利用。
AI.motivation: 随着数据集规模扩大，如何高效筛选对SSL最有价值的数据成为关键问题，传统方法缺乏有效量化标准。
AI.method: 理论证明相似增强数据对SSL贡献最大，并通过实验验证可安全移除CIFAR100/STL10/TinyImageNet数据集的20%-40%示例。
AI.result: 所选子集在保持下游任务性能的同时优于随机子集3%以上，且SSL最优子集与监督学习最优子集呈现负相关。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为SSL提供数据高效解决方案，揭示了SSL与监督学习在数据价值评估上的本质差异。

title: Video Action Recognition Collaborative Learning with Dynamics via PSO-ConvNet Transformer
title_zh: 通过 PSO-ConvNet Transformer 进行视频动作识别动态协作学习
abstract: Recognizing human actions in video sequences, known as Human Action Recognition (HAR), is a challenging task in pattern recognition. While Convolutional Neural Networks (ConvNets) have shown remarkable success in image recognition, they are not always directly applicable to HAR, as temporal features are critical for accurate classification. In this paper, we propose a novel dynamic PSO-ConvNet model for learning actions in videos, building on our recent work in image recognition. Our approach leverages a framework where the weight vector of each neural network represents the position of a particle in phase space, and particles share their current weight vectors and gradient estimates of the Loss function. To extend our approach to video, we integrate ConvNets with state-of-the-art temporal methods such as Transformer and Recurrent Neural Networks. Our experimental results on the UCF-101 dataset demonstrate substantial improvements of up to 9% in accuracy, which confirms the effectiveness of our proposed method. In addition, we conducted experiments on larger and more variety of datasets including Kinetics-400 and HMDB-51 and obtained preference for Collaborative Learning in comparison with Non-Collaborative Learning (Individual Learning). Overall, our dynamic PSO-ConvNet model provides a promising direction for improving HAR by better capturing the spatio-temporal dynamics of human actions in videos. The code is available at https://github.com/leonlha/Video-Action-Recognition-Collaborative-Learning-with-Dynamics-via-PSO-ConvNet-Transformer.
summary: Recognizing human actions in video sequences, known as Human Action Recognition (HAR), is a challenging task in pattern recognition. While Convolutional Neural Networks (ConvNets) have shown remarkable success in image recognition, they are not always directly applicable to HAR, as temporal features are critical for accurate classification. In this paper, we propose a novel dynamic PSO-ConvNet model for learning actions in videos, building on our recent work in image recognition. Our approach leverages a framework where the weight vector of each neural network represents the position of a particle in phase space, and particles share their current weight vectors and gradient estimates of the Loss function. To extend our approach to video, we integrate ConvNets with state-of-the-art temporal methods such as Transformer and Recurrent Neural Networks. Our experimental results on the UCF-101 dataset demonstrate substantial improvements of up to 9% in accuracy, which confirms the effectiveness of our proposed method. In addition, we conducted experiments on larger and more variety of datasets including Kinetics-400 and HMDB-51 and obtained preference for Collaborative Learning in comparison with Non-Collaborative Learning (Individual Learning). Overall, our dynamic PSO-ConvNet model provides a promising direction for improving HAR by better capturing the spatio-temporal dynamics of human actions in videos. The code is available at https://github.com/leonlha/Video-Action-Recognition-Collaborative-Learning-with-Dynamics-via-PSO-ConvNet-Transformer.
abstract_zh: 识别视频序列中的人类动作，称为人类动作识别 (HAR)，是模式识别中的一项具有挑战性的任务。虽然卷积神经网络 (ConvNet) 在图像识别方面取得了显着的成功，但它们并不总是直接适用于 HAR，因为时间特征对于准确分类至关重要。在本文中，我们基于我们最近在图像识别方面的工作，提出了一种新颖的动态 PSO-ConvNet 模型，用于学习视频中的动作。我们的方法利用了一个框架，其中每个神经网络的权重向量表示粒子在相空间中的位置，并且粒子共享其当前的权重向量和损失函数的梯度估计。为了将我们的方法扩展到视频领域，我们将 ConvNet 与最先进的时间方法（例如 Transformer 和循环神经网络）集成。我们在 UCF-101 数据集上的实验结果表明，准确率大幅提高了 9%，这证实了我们提出的方法的有效性。此外，我们在包括 Kinetics-400 和 HMDB-51 在内的更大、更多样化的数据集上进行了实验，并获得了与非协作学习（个人学习）相比协作学习的偏好。总的来说，我们的动态 PSO-ConvNet 模型通过更好地捕捉视频中人类行为的时空动态，为改进 HAR 提供了一个有前途的方向。该代码位于 https://github.com/leonlha/Video-Action-Recognition-Collaborative-Learning-with-Dynamics-via-PSO-ConvNet-Transformer。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合粒子群优化（PSO）与卷积神经网络（ConvNet）及Transformer的动态模型，用于改进视频中的人类行为识别（HAR）。
AI.motivation: 传统卷积神经网络（ConvNet）在图像识别中表现优异，但难以直接应用于视频动作识别，因其缺乏对时间特征的有效建模。本文旨在通过引入动态协作学习机制提升视频动作识别的准确性。
AI.method: 提出动态PSO-ConvNet模型，将每个神经网络的权重向量视为相空间中的粒子，通过粒子间共享权重和损失函数梯度估计实现协作学习，并结合Transformer和循环神经网络（RNN）处理时间信息。
AI.result: 在UCF-101、Kinetics-400和HMDB-51数据集上实验表明，该方法准确率提升最高达9%，且协作学习优于非协作学习。
AI.conclusion: 动态PSO-ConvNet模型通过有效捕捉视频中人类动作的时空动态特性，为改进视频动作识别提供了可行方向。
